title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: BayesianQDM package provides comprehensive framework Bayesian Quantitative Decision-Making (QDM) clinical trials. implements three-zone Go/NoGo/Gray decision rule two complementary probability metrics: Posterior probability: quantifies evidence observed PoC data treatment effect exceeds clinically meaningful threshold. Posterior predictive probability: quantifies probability future (Phase III) trial succeed given observed PoC results. metrics available single two-endpoint analyses, covering binary continuous outcomes, across three study designs (controlled, uncontrolled, external control). package also provides grid-search functions (getgamma family) find optimal Go/NoGo thresholds satisfying user-specified operating characteristic criteria.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: Let πj\pi_j denote response rate group jj (j=1j = 1: treatment, j=2j = 2: control). place independent Beta priors rate: πj∼Beta(aj,bj).\pi_j \sim \mathrm{Beta}(a_j,\, b_j). Given njn_j patients yjy_j responders, posterior πj∣yj∼Beta(aj+yj,bj+nj−yj).\pi_j \mid y_j \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}\!\left(a_j + y_j,\; b_j + n_j - y_j\right). treatment effect interest difference θ=π1−π2\theta = \pi_1 - \pi_2. two groups independent, posterior θ\theta distribution difference two independent Beta random variables, closed-form CDF. package evaluates P(θ>θ0∣𝐲)=P(π1−π2>θ0∣𝐲)P(\theta > \theta_0 \mid \mathbf{y}) = P(\pi_1 - \pi_2 > \theta_0 \mid \mathbf{y}) numerical integration via pbetadiff(): P(π1−π2>θ0)=∫max(0,θ0)1fBeta(a1*,b1*)(u)[1−FBeta(a2*,b2*)(u−θ0)]du,P(\pi_1 - \pi_2 > \theta_0) = \int_{\max(0,\,\theta_0)}^{1}   f_{\mathrm{Beta}(a_1^*, b_1^*)}(u)\;   \bigl[1 - F_{\mathrm{Beta}(a_2^*, b_2^*)}(u - \theta_0)\bigr]\, du, aj*=aj+yja_j^* = a_j + y_j bj*=bj+nj−yjb_j^* = b_j + n_j - y_j posterior shape parameters.

text: Let ỹj∣πj∼Binomial(mj,πj)\tilde{y}_j \mid \pi_j \sim \mathrm{Binomial}(m_j, \pi_j) denote future trial outcomes group jj future sample size mjm_j. Integrating unknown response rates posteriors gives posterior predictive (Beta-Binomial) distribution: P(ỹj=k)=(mjk)B(aj*+k,bj*+mj−k)B(aj*,bj*),k=0,1,…,mj,P(\tilde{y}_j = k) = \binom{m_j}{k}   \frac{B(a_j^* + k,\; b_j^* + m_j - k)}{B(a_j^*,\, b_j^*)},   \quad k = 0, 1, \ldots, m_j, B(⋅,⋅)B(\cdot,\cdot) Beta function. posterior predictive probability successful future trial P(θ̃>θ0∣𝐲)=∑k1=0m1∑k2=0m2𝟏[k1/m1−k2/m2>θ0]P(ỹ1=k1)P(ỹ2=k2),P\!\left(\tilde{\theta} > \theta_0 \mid \mathbf{y}\right) =   \sum_{k_1=0}^{m_1}\sum_{k_2=0}^{m_2}   \mathbf{1}[k_1/m_1 - k_2/m_2 > \theta_0]\;   P(\tilde{y}_1 = k_1)\; P(\tilde{y}_2 = k_2), evaluated exactly pbetabinomdiff().

text: predictive distribution single future observation ỹj\tilde{y}_j N-Inv-χ2\chi^2 posterior non-standardised tt-distribution. future sample mean ỹ‾j\bar{\tilde{y}}_j based mjm_j future patients, scale reduced mj\sqrt{m_j}: ỹ‾j∣𝐲∼tνnj(μnj,σnj1+κnjκnjmj).\bar{\tilde{y}}_j \mid \mathbf{y} \;\sim\;   t_{\nu_{nj}}\!\left(\mu_{nj},\;   \sigma_{nj}\sqrt{\frac{1 + \kappa_{nj}}{\kappa_{nj}\, m_j}}\right). vague prior (κ0j=ν0j=0\kappa_{0j} = \nu_{0j} = 0, κnj=nj\kappa_{nj} = n_j, σnj=sj\sigma_{nj} = s_j) becomes ỹ‾j∣𝐲∼tnj−1(y‾j,sjnj+1njmj).\bar{\tilde{y}}_j \mid \mathbf{y} \;\sim\;   t_{n_j - 1}\!\left(\bar{y}_j,\;   s_j\sqrt{\frac{n_j + 1}{n_j\, m_j}}\right). posterior predictive probability future treatment effect exceeding θ0\theta_0 P(ỹ‾1−ỹ‾2>θ0∣𝐲),P\!\left(\bar{\tilde{y}}_1 - \bar{\tilde{y}}_2 > \theta_0   \mid \mathbf{y}\right), computed three methods posterior probability (Section 3), predictive scale replacing posterior scale.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: core computational challenge evaluating CDF difference D=T1−T2D = T_1 - T_2 two independent non-standardised tt-variables Tj∼tνj(μj,σj),j=1,2,T_j \sim t_{\nu_j}(\mu_j, \sigma_j), \qquad j = 1, 2, threshold θ0\theta_0. Three methods available.

text: Independent samples drawn marginal distribution: T1()∼tν1(μ1,σ1),T2()∼tν2(μ2,σ2),=1,…,nMC,T_1^{()} \sim t_{\nu_1}(\mu_1, \sigma_1), \quad   T_2^{()} \sim t_{\nu_2}(\mu_2, \sigma_2), \quad   = 1, \ldots, n_{\mathrm{MC}}, probability estimated empirical proportion: P̂(D>θ0)=1nMC∑=1nMC𝟏[T1()−T2()>θ0].\widehat{P}(D > \theta_0) =   \frac{1}{n_{\mathrm{MC}}}   \sum_{=1}^{n_{\mathrm{MC}}} \mathbf{1}\!\left[T_1^{()} - T_2^{()} > \theta_0\right]. package implements ptdiff_MC() using vectorised approach: called pbayesdecisionprob1cont() vector nsimn_{\mathrm{sim}} scenarios, draws generated single nMC×nsimn_{\mathrm{MC}} \times n_{\mathrm{sim}} matrix avoid repeated loop overhead.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: concurrent control arm available (single-arm PoC trial), control distribution specified external knowledge rather observed data. Binary endpoint: hypothetical responder count zz specified control group size n2n_2, yielding posterior shape parameters a2*=a2+za_2^* = a_2 + z b2*=b2+n2−zb_2^* = b_2 + n_2 - z. Continuous endpoint: hypothetical control mean μ02\mu_{02} fixed prior knowledge control variance scaled relative treatment posterior variance via scaling factor r>0r > 0: σcontrol=r⋅σtreatment,\sigma_{\mathrm{control}} = \sqrt{r} \cdot \sigma_{\mathrm{treatment}}, scale control tt-distribution r\sqrt{r} times treatment.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: Historical external data incorporated power prior borrowing weight αe∈(0,1]\alpha_e \(0, 1]. Beta-Binomial model group jj, power prior augments initial prior fraction αej\alpha_{ej} external data sufficient statistics: (αj*,βj*)=(aj+αejyej,bj+αej(nej−yej)),(\alpha_j^*,\; \beta_j^*) =   \left(a_j + \alpha_{ej}\, y_{ej},\;\;   b_j + \alpha_{ej}(n_{ej} - y_{ej})\right), nejn_{ej} yejy_{ej} external sample size responder count group jj. augmented parameters serve prior current PoC data, yielding closed-form posterior without MCMC. N-Inv-χ2\chi^2 model, power prior inflates prior precision degrees freedom weighted external sample: κ0j*=αejnej,ν0j*=αejnej−1,\kappa_{0j}^* = \alpha_{ej}\, n_{ej}, \quad   \nu_{0j}^* = \alpha_{ej}\, n_{ej} - 1, μ0j*=y‾ej,σ0j*2=sej2,\mu_{0j}^* = \bar{y}_{ej}, \quad   \sigma_{0j}^{*2} = s_{ej}^2, leading posterior updates μnj=αejnejy‾ej+njy‾jαejnej+nj,\mu_{nj} = \frac{\alpha_{ej} n_{ej}\, \bar{y}_{ej} + n_j\, \bar{y}_j}   {\alpha_{ej} n_{ej} + n_j}, σnj2=αej(nej−1)sej2+(nj−1)sj2+αejnejnj(y‾ej−y‾j)2αejnej+njαejnej+nj,\sigma_{nj}^2 = \frac{\alpha_{ej}(n_{ej}-1) s_{ej}^2 + (n_j-1) s_j^2 +   \dfrac{\alpha_{ej} n_{ej} n_j (\bar{y}_{ej} - \bar{y}_j)^2}         {\alpha_{ej} n_{ej} + n_j}}   {\alpha_{ej} n_{ej} + n_j}, degrees freedom νnj=αejnej+nj−1\nu_{nj} = \alpha_{ej} n_{ej} + n_j - 1. Setting αej=1\alpha_{ej} = 1 corresponds full borrowing; αej→0\alpha_{ej} \0 recovers vague-prior result current data alone.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: Go/NoGo/Gray decision rule uses two pre-specified thresholds γ1>γ2\gamma_1 > \gamma_2 applied pair probabilities (gGo,gNoGo)(g_{\mathrm{Go}},\, g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}): Decision={𝐆𝐨gGo≥γ1 gNoGo<γ2𝐍𝐨𝐆𝐨gGo<γ1 gNoGo≥γ2𝐌𝐢𝐬𝐬gGo≥γ1 gNoGo≥γ2𝐆𝐫𝐚𝐲otherwise (neither Go NoGo criterion met).\text{Decision} = \begin{cases}   \textbf{Go}   & \text{} g_{\mathrm{Go}}   \ge \gamma_1 \text{ }                               g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} <  \gamma_2 \\   \textbf{NoGo} & \text{} g_{\mathrm{Go}}   <  \gamma_1 \text{ }                               g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_2 \\   \textbf{Miss} & \text{} g_{\mathrm{Go}}   \ge \gamma_1 \text{ }                               g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_2 \\   \textbf{Gray} & \text{otherwise (neither Go NoGo criterion met).} \end{cases} Miss occurs Go NoGo criteria simultaneously satisfied, indicates inconsistent threshold configuration. default functions stop error Miss probability >0> 0 (error_if_Miss = TRUE). posterior probability, θTV\theta_{\mathrm{TV}} (Target Value) θMAV\theta_{\mathrm{MAV}} (Minimum Acceptable Value, θTV>θMAV\theta_{\mathrm{TV}} > \theta_{\mathrm{MAV}}) define two separate probabilities: gGo=P(θ>θTV∣𝐲),gNoGo=P(θ≤θMAV∣𝐲).g_{\mathrm{Go}} = P(\theta > \theta_{\mathrm{TV}} \mid \mathbf{y}), \qquad   g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} = P(\theta \le \theta_{\mathrm{MAV}} \mid \mathbf{y}). posterior predictive probability, single null threshold θNULL\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} used probability serves roles: gGo=gNoGo=P(θ̃>θNULL∣𝐲),g_{\mathrm{Go}} = g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} =   P(\tilde{\theta} > \theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} \mid \mathbf{y}), decision reduces Go g≥γ1g \ge \gamma_1, NoGo g<γ2g < \gamma_2, Gray otherwise (Miss occur case).

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: given true parameter value (e.g., true treatment effect), operating characteristics marginal probabilities decision outcome: Pr(Go)=∑𝐲𝟏[decision(𝐲)=Go]P(𝐲∣true parameter),\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \sum_{\mathbf{y}} \mathbf{1}[\mathrm{decision}(\mathbf{y}) = \mathrm{Go}]\;   P(\mathbf{y} \mid \text{true parameter}), analogously Pr(NoGo)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) Pr(Gray)\Pr(\mathrm{Gray}), 𝐲\mathbf{y} denotes observed data sum (integral continuous outcomes) taken possible outcomes true parameter. binary endpoints, exact marginal probabilities possible outcome pairs (y1,y2)∈{0,…,n1}×{0,…,n2}(y_1, y_2) \\{0,\ldots,n_1\} \times \{0,\ldots,n_2\} computed pbayesdecisionprob1bin() using binomial probability weights: Pr(Go)=∑y1=0n1∑y2=0n2𝟏[gGo(y1,y2)≥γ1 gNoGo(y1,y2)<γ2](n1y1)π1y1(1−π1)n1−y1(n2y2)π2y2(1−π2)n2−y2.\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \sum_{y_1=0}^{n_1} \sum_{y_2=0}^{n_2}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{Go}}(y_1, y_2) \ge \gamma_1     \;\text{ }\; g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}(y_1, y_2) < \gamma_2\right]\;   \binom{n_1}{y_1} \pi_1^{y_1}(1-\pi_1)^{n_1-y_1}\;   \binom{n_2}{y_2} \pi_2^{y_2}(1-\pi_2)^{n_2-y_2}. continuous endpoints, operating characteristics estimated Monte Carlo simulation nsimn_{\mathrm{sim}} replicates pbayesdecisionprob1cont(): replicate ii, dataset (y‾1(),s1(),y‾2(),s2())(\bar{y}_1^{()}, s_1^{()}, \bar{y}_2^{()}, s_2^{()}) generated true parameter distribution, probability g()g^{()} computed, decision recorded. Empirical proportions replicates yield estimated operating characteristics.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: getgamma family functions finds optimal pair (γ1,γ2)(\gamma_1, \gamma_2) grid search user-supplied candidate grid Γ={γ(1),…,γ(K)}⊂(0,1)\Gamma = \{\gamma^{(1)}, \ldots, \gamma^{(K)}\} \subset (0, 1). two-stage precompute--sweep strategy used efficiency: Stage 1 (Precomputation): posterior/predictive probabilities g(y1,y2)g(y_1, y_2) (binary) g()g^{()} (continuous, nsimn_{\mathrm{sim}} replicates) computed , independently γ\gamma. Stage 2 (Gamma sweep): candidate γ∈Γ\gamma \\Gamma, Pr(Go∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma) Pr(NoGo∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma) obtained fast indicator summations precomputed values without additional probability evaluations. optimal γ1\gamma_1 selected smallest (largest) value Γ\Gamma Pr(Go∣γ1)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_1) satisfies user-specified criterion (e.g., Pr(Go)<0.05\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < 0.05 null scenario), γ2\gamma_2 selected analogously NoGo criterion.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: two endpoints evaluated jointly, treatment effect vector 𝛉=(θ1,θ2)\boldsymbol{\theta} = (\theta_1, \theta_2). endpoint k=1,2k = 1, 2, three zones defined θTVk\theta_{\mathrm{TV}k} θMAVk\theta_{\mathrm{MAV}k}: Go zone: θk>θTVk\theta_k > \theta_{\mathrm{TV}k} Gray zone: θMAVk<θk≤θTVk\theta_{\mathrm{MAV}k} < \theta_k \le \theta_{\mathrm{TV}k} NoGo zone: θk≤θMAVk\theta_k \le \theta_{\mathrm{MAV}k} Cartesian product defines nine mutually exclusive regions R1,…,R9R_1, \ldots, R_9: Nine-region grid two-endpoint posterior probability Region R1R_1 (endpoints exceed θTV\theta_{\mathrm{TV}}) typically designated Go R9R_9 (endpoints θMAV\theta_{\mathrm{MAV}}) NoGo.

what: 6.3 Joint Posterior for Two Binary Endpoints
title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: two binary endpoints response rates (π1(1),π2(1))(\pi_1^{(1)}, \pi_2^{(1)}) (endpoint 1) (π1(2),π2(2))(\pi_1^{(2)}, \pi_2^{(2)}) (endpoint 2), Dirichlet-multinomial model used. treatment arm size n1n_1, define four mutually exclusive categories (respond, endpoint 1 responds, endpoint 2 responds, neither responds) probabilities 𝐩1=(p11,p10,p01,p00)\mathbf{p}_1 = (p_{11}, p_{10}, p_{01}, p_{00}). Dirichlet prior Dir(𝐚1)\mathrm{Dir}(\mathbf{}_1) observed multinomial counts 𝐱1\mathbf{x}_1 yield conjugate posterior 𝐩1∣𝐱1∼Dir(𝐚1+𝐱1).\mathbf{p}_1 \mid \mathbf{x}_1 \;\sim\;   \mathrm{Dir}(\mathbf{}_1 + \mathbf{x}_1). joint probabilities nine regions computed Monte Carlo draws Dirichlet posteriors pbayespostpred2bin().

what: 6.4 Joint Posterior for Two Continuous Endpoints
title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: two continuous endpoints, Normal-Inverse-Wishart (N-Inv-W) conjugate prior placed (𝛍j,𝚺j)(\boldsymbol{\mu}_j, \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_j): (𝛍j,𝚺j)∼N-Inv-W(𝐦0j,κ0j,ν0j,𝐕0j).(\boldsymbol{\mu}_j, \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_j) \;\sim\;   \mathrm{N\text{-}Inv\text{-}W}(\mathbf{m}_{0j},\, \kappa_{0j},\,   \nu_{0j},\, \mathbf{V}_{0j}). Given njn_j bivariate observations, posterior parameters updated analogously univariate case, yielding matrix-tt predictive distribution. joint probability rectangular regions evaluated using bivariate tt-distribution via mvtnorm::pmvt() MC method, Moment-Matching approximation MM method (Theorem 3 Homma Yamaguchi, 2025).

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
code: # Operating characteristics for single binary endpoint pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob      = 'posterior',   design    = 'controlled',   theta.TV  = 0.20,  theta.MAV = 0.05,  theta.NULL = NULL,   gamma1    = 0.80,  gamma2    = 0.20,   pi1       = c(0.15, 0.30, 0.50),   pi2       = rep(0.15, 3),   n1 = 10,  n2 = 10,   a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   z = NULL, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior #>   Design           : controlled #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 0.2, MAV = 0.05 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.2 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 10, n2 = 10 #>   Prior (Beta)     : a1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5 #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   pi1  pi2     Go   Gray   NoGo #>  0.15 0.15 0.0137 0.0963 0.8900 #>  0.30 0.15 0.1357 0.2016 0.6627 #>  0.50 0.15 0.5081 0.2026 0.2893 #> ------------------------------------------------------------

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: use hypothetical Phase IIa proof--concept (PoC) trial moderate--severe rheumatoid arthritis (RA). investigational drug compared placebo 1:1 randomised controlled trial n1=n2=12n_1 = n_2 = 12 patients per arm. Endpoint: ACR20 response rate (proportion patients achieving ≥20%\ge 20\% improvement ACR criteria). Clinically meaningful thresholds (posterior probability): Target Value (TV) = 0.20 (treatment–control difference) Minimum Acceptable Value (MAV) = 0.05 Null hypothesis threshold (predictive probability): θNULL=0.10\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} = 0.10. Decision thresholds: γ1=0.80\gamma_1 = 0.80 (Go), γ2=0.20\gamma_2 = 0.20 (NoGo). Observed data (used Sections 2–4): y1=8y_1 = 8 responders n1=12n_1 = 12 (treatment); y2=3y_2 = 3 responders n2=12n_2 = 12 (control).

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: Let πj\pi_j denote true response rate group jj (j=1j = 1: treatment, j=2j = 2: control). model number responders yj∣πj∼Binomial(nj,πj),y_j \mid \pi_j \;\sim\; \mathrm{Binomial}(n_j,\, \pi_j), place independent Beta priors rate: πj∼Beta(aj,bj),\pi_j \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_j,\, b_j), aj>0a_j > 0 bj>0b_j > 0 shape parameters. Jeffreys prior corresponds aj=bj=0.5a_j = b_j = 0.5, giving weakly informative, symmetric prior (0,1)(0, 1). uniform prior corresponds aj=bj=1a_j = b_j = 1.

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: conjugacy Beta-Binomial model, posterior observing yjy_j responders among njn_j patients πj∣yj∼Beta(aj*,bj*),\pi_j \mid y_j \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_j^*,\, b_j^*), posterior shape parameters aj*=aj+yj,bj*=bj+nj−yj.a_j^* = a_j + y_j, \qquad b_j^* = b_j + n_j - y_j. posterior mean variance E[πj∣yj]=aj*aj*+bj*,Var(πj∣yj)=aj*bj*(aj*+bj*)2(aj*+bj*+1).E[\pi_j \mid y_j] = \frac{a_j^*}{a_j^* + b_j^*}, \qquad   \mathrm{Var}(\pi_j \mid y_j) =   \frac{a_j^*\, b_j^*}{(a_j^* + b_j^*)^2\,(a_j^* + b_j^* + 1)}.

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: treatment effect θ=π1−π2\theta = \pi_1 - \pi_2. Since two groups independent, θ∣𝐲\theta \mid \mathbf{y} follows distribution difference two independent Beta random variables: π1∣y1∼Beta(a1*,b1*),π2∣y2∼Beta(a2*,b2*).\pi_1 \mid y_1 \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_1^*,\, b_1^*), \qquad   \pi_2 \mid y_2 \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_2^*,\, b_2^*). closed-form CDF θ=π1−π2\theta = \pi_1 - \pi_2, posterior probability P(θ>θ0∣𝐲)=P(π1−π2>θ0∣𝐲)P(\theta > \theta_0 \mid \mathbf{y}) = P(\pi_1 - \pi_2 > \theta_0   \mid \mathbf{y}) evaluated convolution integral implemented pbetadiff(): P(π1−π2>θ0)=∫01FBeta(a2*,b2*)(x−θ0)fBeta(a1*,b1*)(x)dx,P(\pi_1 - \pi_2 > \theta_0) =   \int_0^1   F_{\mathrm{Beta}(a_2^*, b_2^*)}\!\left(x - \theta_0\right)\,   f_{\mathrm{Beta}(a_1^*, b_1^*)}\!(x)\; dx, FBeta(α,β)F_{\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha,\beta)} Beta CDF fBeta(α,β)f_{\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha,\beta)} Beta PDF. one-dimensional integral evaluated adaptive Gauss-Kronrod quadrature via stats::integrate().

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: Let ỹj∣πj∼Binomial(mj,πj)\tilde{y}_j \mid \pi_j \sim \mathrm{Binomial}(m_j, \pi_j) future trial counts mjm_j future patients. Integrating πj\pi_j Beta posterior gives Beta-Binomial predictive distribution: P(ỹj=k∣yj)=(mjk)B(aj*+k,bj*+mj−k)B(aj*,bj*),k=0,1,…,mj,P(\tilde{y}_j = k \mid y_j) =   \binom{m_j}{k}   \frac{B(a_j^* + k,\; b_j^* + m_j - k)}{B(a_j^*,\; b_j^*)},   \quad k = 0, 1, \ldots, m_j, B(⋅,⋅)B(\cdot, \cdot) Beta function.

what: 2.2 Posterior Predictive Probability of Future Success
title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: posterior predictive probability future treatment difference exceeds θNULL\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} P(ỹ1m1−ỹ2m2>θNULL|𝐲)=∑k1=0m1∑k2=0m2𝟏[k1m1−k2m2>θNULL]P(ỹ1=k1∣y1)P(ỹ2=k2∣y2).P\!\left(\frac{\tilde{y}_1}{m_1} - \frac{\tilde{y}_2}{m_2}   > \theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} \;\Big|\; \mathbf{y}\right)   = \sum_{k_1=0}^{m_1} \sum_{k_2=0}^{m_2}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\frac{k_1}{m_1} - \frac{k_2}{m_2} >   \theta_{\mathrm{NULL}}\right]\,   P(\tilde{y}_1 = k_1 \mid y_1)\,   P(\tilde{y}_2 = k_2 \mid y_2). double sum (m1+1)(m2+1)(m_1 + 1)(m_2 + 1) outcome combinations evaluated exactly pbetabinomdiff().

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: Standard parallel-group RCT observed data arms. posterior shape parameters group jj aj*=aj+yj,bj*=bj+nj−yj,a_j^* = a_j + y_j, \qquad b_j^* = b_j + n_j - y_j, posterior probability P(π1−π2>θ0∣𝐲)P(\pi_1 - \pi_2 > \theta_0 \mid \mathbf{y}) computed via pbetadiff(). Posterior probability TV MAV: Posterior predictive probability (future Phase III: m1=m2=40m_1 = m_2 = 40): Vectorised computation possible outcomes: function accepts vectors y1 y2, enabling efficient computation posterior probability every possible outcome pair (y1,y2)∈{0,…,n1}×{0,…,n2}(y_1, y_2) \\{0,\ldots,n_1\} \times \{0,\ldots,n_2\}.
code:
# P(pi1 - pi2 > TV  | data) p_tv <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', theta0 = 0.20,   n1 = 12, n2 = 12, y1 = 8, y2 = 3,   a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL, z = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE )  # P(pi1 - pi2 > MAV | data) p_mav <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', theta0 = 0.05,   n1 = 12, n2 = 12, y1 = 8, y2 = 3,   a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL, z = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE )  cat(sprintf("P(theta > TV  | data) = %.4f  -> Go  criterion (>= %.2f): %s
",             p_tv,  0.80, ifelse(p_tv  >= 0.80, "YES", "NO"))) #> P(theta > TV  | data) = 0.8517  -> Go  criterion (>= 0.80): YES cat(sprintf("P(theta <= MAV | data) = %.4f  -> NoGo criterion (>= %.2f): %s
",             1 - p_mav, 0.20, ifelse((1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "YES", "NO"))) #> P(theta <= MAV | data) = 0.0347  -> NoGo criterion (>= 0.20): NO cat(sprintf("Decision: %s
",             ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) < 0.20, "Go",                    ifelse(p_tv < 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "NoGo",                           ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "Miss", "Gray"))))) #> Decision: Go p_pred <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled', theta0 = 0.10,   n1 = 12, n2 = 12, y1 = 8, y2 = 3,   a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   m1 = 40, m2 = 40, z = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("Predictive probability (m1 = m2 = 40) = %.4f
", p_pred)) #> Predictive probability (m1 = m2 = 40) = 0.9053 grid <- expand.grid(y1 = 0:12, y2 = 0:12) p_all <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', theta0 = 0.20,   n1 = 12, n2 = 12, y1 = grid$y1, y2 = grid$y2,   a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL, z = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) # Show results for a selection of outcome pairs sel <- data.frame(y1 = grid$y1, y2 = grid$y2, P_gt_TV = round(p_all, 4)) head(sel[order(-sel$P_gt_TV), ], 10) #>    y1 y2 P_gt_TV #> 12 11  0  1.0000 #> 13 12  0  1.0000 #> 26 12  1  1.0000 #> 11 10  0  0.9998 #> 39 12  2  0.9998 #> 25 11  1  0.9997 #> 10  9  0  0.9992 #> 52 12  3  0.9992 #> 24 10  1  0.9982 #> 38 11  2  0.9982

text: concurrent control arm enrolled, hypothetical control responder count zz specified historical knowledge. control posterior π2∣z∼Beta(a2+z,b2+n2−z),\pi_2 \mid z \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_2 + z,\; b_2 + n_2 - z), n2n_2 hypothetical control sample size zz assumed number hypothetical responders (0≤z≤n20 \le z \le n_2). treatment arm data y1y_1 observed; y2y_2 set NULL. Effect hypothetical control count: varying zz shows sensitivity assumed control rate.
code: # Hypothetical control: z = 2 responders out of n2 = 12 p_unctrl <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled', theta0 = 0.20,   n1 = 12, n2 = 12, y1 = 8, y2 = NULL,   a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL, z = 2L,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > TV | data, uncontrolled, z=2) = %.4f\n", p_unctrl)) #> P(theta > TV | data, uncontrolled, z=2) = 0.9338 z_seq <- 0:12 p_z   <- sapply(z_seq, function(z) {   pbayespostpred1bin(     prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled', theta0 = 0.20,     n1 = 12, n2 = 12, y1 = 8, y2 = NULL,     a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,     m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL, z = z,     ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,     lower.tail = FALSE   ) }) data.frame(z = z_seq, P_gt_TV = round(p_z, 4)) #>     z P_gt_TV #> 1   0  0.9968 #> 2   1  0.9787 #> 3   2  0.9338 #> 4   3  0.8517 #> 5   4  0.7297 #> 6   5  0.5760 #> 7   6  0.4099 #> 8   7  0.2558 #> 9   8  0.1350 #> 10  9  0.0571 #> 11 10  0.0177 #> 12 11  0.0034 #> 13 12  0.0002

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: Historical external data incorporated via power prior borrowing weight αej∈(0,1]\alpha_{ej} \(0, 1]. augmented prior shape parameters αj*=aj+αejyej,βj*=bj+αej(nej−yej),\alpha_j^* = a_j + \alpha_{ej}\, y_{ej}, \qquad   \beta_j^* = b_j + \alpha_{ej}\,(n_{ej} - y_{ej}), nejn_{ej} yejy_{ej} external sample size responder count group jj. serve prior current PoC data, yielding closed-form posterior: πj∣yj∼Beta(αj*+yj,βj*+nj−yj).\pi_j \mid y_j \;\sim\;   \mathrm{Beta}\!\left(\alpha_j^* + y_j,\; \beta_j^* + n_j - y_j\right). Setting αej=1\alpha_{ej} = 1 corresponds full borrowing (external data treated came current trial); αej→0\alpha_{ej} \0 recovers result current data alone original prior. , external data arms: ne1=15n_{e1} = 15, ye1=5y_{e1} = 5, ne2=15n_{e2} = 15, ye2=4y_{e2} = 4, borrowing weights αe1=αe2=0.5\alpha_{e1} = \alpha_{e2} = 0.5: Effect borrowing weight: varying αe2\alpha_{e2} (control arm ) fixed αe1=0.5\alpha_{e1} = 0.5:
code: p_ext <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', theta0 = 0.20,   n1 = 12, n2 = 12, y1 = 8, y2 = 3,   a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL, z = NULL,   ne1 = 15L, ne2 = 15L, ye1 = 5L, ye2 = 4L, ae1 = 0.5, ae2 = 0.5,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > TV | data, external, ae=0.5) = %.4f\n", p_ext)) #> P(theta > TV | data, external, ae=0.5) = 0.6874 # ae must be in (0, 1]; avoid ae = 0 ae_seq <- c(0.01, seq(0.1, 1.0, by = 0.1)) p_ae   <- sapply(ae_seq, function(ae) {   pbayespostpred1bin(     prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', theta0 = 0.20,     n1 = 12, n2 = 12, y1 = 8, y2 = 3,     a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,     m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL, z = NULL,     ne1 = 15L, ne2 = 15L, ye1 = 5L, ye2 = 4L, ae1 = 0.5, ae2 = ae,     lower.tail = FALSE   ) }) data.frame(ae2 = ae_seq, P_gt_TV = round(p_ae, 4)) #>     ae2 P_gt_TV #> 1  0.01  0.6735 #> 2  0.10  0.6766 #> 3  0.20  0.6797 #> 4  0.30  0.6825 #> 5  0.40  0.6851 #> 6  0.50  0.6874 #> 7  0.60  0.6896 #> 8  0.70  0.6916 #> 9  0.80  0.6934 #> 10 0.90  0.6951 #> 11 1.00  0.6967

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: given true response rates (π1,π2)(\pi_1, \pi_2), operating characteristics computed exact enumeration (n1+1)(n2+1)(n_1 + 1)(n_2 + 1) possible outcome pairs (y1,y2)∈{0,…,n1}×{0,…,n2}(y_1, y_2) \\{0,\ldots,n_1\} \times \{0,\ldots,n_2\}: Pr(Go)=∑y1=0n1∑y2=0n2𝟏[gGo(y1,y2)≥γ1 gNoGo(y1,y2)<γ2](n1y1)π1y1(1−π1)n1−y1(n2y2)π2y2(1−π2)n2−y2,\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) =   \sum_{y_1=0}^{n_1} \sum_{y_2=0}^{n_2}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{Go}}(y_1, y_2) \ge \gamma_1     \;\text{ }\; g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}(y_1, y_2) < \gamma_2\right]\,   \binom{n_1}{y_1} \pi_1^{y_1}(1-\pi_1)^{n_1-y_1}\,   \binom{n_2}{y_2} \pi_2^{y_2}(1-\pi_2)^{n_2-y_2}, gGo(y1,y2)=P(π1−π2>θTV∣y1,y2),g_{\mathrm{Go}}(y_1, y_2) = P(\pi_1 - \pi_2 > \theta_{\mathrm{TV}} \mid y_1, y_2),gNoGo(y1,y2)=P(π1−π2≤θMAV∣y1,y2),g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}(y_1, y_2) = P(\pi_1 - \pi_2 \le \theta_{\mathrm{MAV}} \mid y_1, y_2), decision outcomes classified : Go: gGo≥γ1g_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_1 gNoGo<γ2g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} < \gamma_2 NoGo: gGo<γ1g_{\mathrm{Go}} < \gamma_1 gNoGo≥γ2g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_2 Miss: gGo≥γ1g_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_1 gNoGo≥γ2g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_2 Gray: neither Go NoGo criterion met

text: Operating characteristics (Go/NoGo/Gray) function true treatment effect (pi1 - pi2). Controlled design, Jeffreys prior, exact enumeration. True pi2 = 0.10, n1 = n2 = 12, gamma1 = 0.80, gamma2 = 0.20.
code: pi1_seq <- seq(0.05, 0.75, by = 0.05)  oc_grid <- pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob      = 'posterior',   design    = 'controlled',   theta.TV  = 0.20,  theta.MAV = 0.05,  theta.NULL = NULL,   gamma1    = 0.80,  gamma2    = 0.20,   pi1       = pi1_seq,   pi2       = rep(0.10, length(pi1_seq)),   n1 = 12,  n2 = 12,   a1 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   z = NULL, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE )  delta <- oc_grid$pi1 - 0.10   # true treatment effect  old_par <- par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 1))  plot(delta, oc_grid$Go,      type = "b", pch = 16, lwd = 2, col = "#2166AC",      ylim = c(0, 1),      xlab = expression(pi[1] - pi[2]),      ylab = "Probability",      main = "Operating Characteristics: Controlled Design (Posterior Prob.)",      las  = 1) lines(delta, oc_grid$NoGo,       type = "b", pch = 17, lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D") lines(delta, oc_grid$Gray,       type = "b", pch = 15, lwd = 2, col = "#878787")  abline(h = c(0.20, 0.80), lty = 2, col = "gray50") abline(v = c(0.05, 0.20), lty = 3, col = "gray70")  text(0.05 + 0.01, 0.04, "MAV", col = "gray40", cex = 0.8, adj = 0) text(0.20 + 0.01, 0.04, "TV",  col = "gray40", cex = 0.8, adj = 0)  legend("right",        legend = c("Pr(Go)", "Pr(NoGo)", "Pr(Gray)"),        col    = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#878787"),        pch    = c(16, 17, 15), lwd = 2, bty = "n") par(old_par)

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: getgamma1bin() function finds optimal pair (γ1*,γ2*)(\gamma_1^*, \gamma_2^*) grid search Γ={γ(1),…,γ(K)}⊂(0,1)\Gamma = \{\gamma^{(1)}, \ldots, \gamma^{(K)}\} \subset (0, 1) null scenario (π1null,π2null)(\pi_1^{\mathrm{null}}, \pi_2^{\mathrm{null}}). two-stage precompute--sweep strategy used: Stage 1 (Precompute): posterior/predictive probabilities gGo(y1,y2)g_{\mathrm{Go}}(y_1, y_2) gNoGo(y1,y2)g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}(y_1, y_2) computed (n1+1)(n2+1)(n_1 + 1)(n_2 + 1) outcome pairs — independently γ\gamma. Stage 2 (Sweep): candidate γ∈Γ\gamma \\Gamma, Pr(Go∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma) Pr(NoGo∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma) obtained weighted sums indicator functions: Pr(Go∣γ)=∑y1,y2𝟏[gGo(y1,y2)≥γ]w(y1,y2),\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma) = \sum_{y_1, y_2}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{Go}}(y_1, y_2) \ge \gamma\right]\,   w(y_1, y_2), Pr(NoGo∣γ)=∑y1,y2𝟏[gNoGo(y1,y2)≥γ]w(y1,y2),\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma) = \sum_{y_1, y_2}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}(y_1, y_2) \ge \gamma\right]\,   w(y_1, y_2), w(y1,y2)=Binom(y1;n1,π1)Binom(y2;n2,π2)w(y_1, y_2) = \mathrm{Binom}(y_1; n_1, \pi_1)\,\mathrm{Binom}(y_2; n_2, \pi_2) binomial weights null scenario. calls pbayespostpred1bin() required stage. sweep vectorised using outer(), making full grid search computationally negligible Stage 1 complete. optimal γ1*\gamma_1^* smallest (largest, depending sel_go) value Γ\Gamma Pr(Go∣γ1*)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_1^*) satisfies criterion target (e.g., Pr(Go)<0.05\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < 0.05). Analogously γ2*\gamma_2^*.

code: grid_df <- data.frame(   gamma  = res_gamma$gamma_grid,   PrGo   = res_gamma$PrGo_grid,   PrNoGo = res_gamma$PrNoGo_grid )  old_par2 <- par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 1))  plot(grid_df$gamma, grid_df$PrGo,      type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#2166AC",      ylim = c(0, 1),      xlab = expression(gamma),      ylab = "Probability",      main = "Optimal Threshold Search: Pr(Go) and Pr(NoGo) vs. Gamma",      las  = 1) lines(grid_df$gamma, grid_df$PrNoGo,       lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D")  abline(h = 0.05, lty = 2, col = "#2166AC", lwd = 1.2) abline(h = 0.20, lty = 2, col = "#D6604D", lwd = 1.2)  if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma1)) {   abline(v = res_gamma$gamma1, lty = 2, col = "#2166AC", lwd = 2)   text(res_gamma$gamma1 + 0.02, 0.88,        paste0("gamma1* = ", res_gamma$gamma1),        col = "#2166AC", cex = 0.85, adj = 0) } if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma2)) {   abline(v = res_gamma$gamma2, lty = 2, col = "#D6604D", lwd = 2)   text(res_gamma$gamma2 - 0.02, 0.73,        paste0("gamma2* = ", res_gamma$gamma2),        col = "#D6604D", cex = 0.85, adj = 1) }  legend("topright",        legend = c("Pr(Go | gamma)", "Pr(NoGo | gamma)",                   "Target Go = 0.05", "Target NoGo = 0.20"),        col    = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#2166AC", "#D6604D"),        lty    = c(1, 1, 2, 2),        lwd    = c(2, 2, 1.2, 1.2),        bty    = "n", cex = 0.85) par(old_par2)

what: 5.5 Example: External Control Design, Posterior Probability
title: Single Binary Endpoint
Single Binary Endpoint
text: vignette covered single binary endpoint analysis BayesianQDM: Bayesian model: Beta-Binomial conjugate posterior explicit update formulae aj*=aj+yja_j^* = a_j + y_j bj*=bj+nj−yjb_j^* = b_j + n_j - y_j; Jeffreys prior (aj=bj=0.5a_j = b_j = 0.5) recommended weakly informative default. Posterior probability: evaluated via convolution integral pbetadiff() — closed-form CDF exists Beta difference. Posterior predictive probability: evaluated exact enumeration Beta-Binomial double sum pbetabinomdiff(). Three study designs: controlled (arms observed), uncontrolled (hypothetical control count zz fixed prior knowledge), external control (power prior borrowing weight αej\alpha_{ej}, augmenting prior αej\alpha_{ej} times external sufficient statistics). Operating characteristics: computed exact enumeration (n1+1)(n2+1)(n_1+1)(n_2+1) outcome pairs binomial probability weights — simulation required binary endpoint. Optimal threshold search: two-stage precompute--sweep strategy getgamma1bin() using outer() fully vectorised gamma sweep. See vignette("single-continuous") analogous continuous endpoint analysis.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: use hypothetical Phase IIa proof--concept (PoC) trial moderate--severe rheumatoid arthritis (RA). investigational drug compared placebo 1:1 randomised controlled trial n1=n2=15n_1 = n_2 = 15 patients per arm. Endpoint: change baseline DAS28 score (larger decrease indicates greater improvement). Clinically meaningful thresholds (posterior probability): Target Value (θTV\theta_{\mathrm{TV}}) = 1.5 (ambitious target) Minimum Acceptable Value (θMAV\theta_{\mathrm{MAV}}) = 0.5 (lower bar) Null hypothesis threshold (predictive probability): θNULL=1.0\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} = 1.0. Decision thresholds: γ1=0.80\gamma_1 = 0.80 (Go), γ2=0.20\gamma_2 = 0.20 (NoGo). Observed data (used Sections 2–4): y‾1=3.2\bar{y}_1 = 3.2, s1=2.0s_1 = 2.0 (treatment); y‾2=1.1\bar{y}_2 = 1.1, s2=1.8s_2 = 1.8 (control).

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text:
group jj (j=1j = 1: treatment, j=2j = 2: control), model continuous outcome yji∣μj,σj2∼iid𝒩(μj,σj2).y_{ji} \mid \mu_j, \sigma_j^2 \;\overset{iid}{\sim}\;   \mathcal{N}(\mu_j,\, \sigma_j^2). conjugate prior Normal-Inverse-Chi-squared (N-Inv-χ2\chi^2) distribution: (μj,σj2)∼N-Inv-χ2(μ0j,κ0j,ν0j,σ0j2),(\mu_j,\, \sigma_j^2) \;\sim\;   \mathrm{N	ext{-}Inv	ext{-}\chi^2}   \!\left(\mu_{0j},\, \kappa_{0j},\, 
u_{0j},\, \sigma_{0j}^2ight), μ0j\mu_{0j} prior mean, κ0j>0\kappa_{0j} > 0 prior precision (pseudo-sample size), ν0j>0
u_{0j} > 0 prior degrees freedom, σ0j2>0\sigma_{0j}^2 > 0 prior scale. Vague (Jeffreys) prior obtained letting κ0j→0\kappa_{0j} \0 ν0j→0
u_{0j} \0, places minimal information parameters.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
Single Continuous Endpoint
text: treatment effect θ=μ1−μ2\theta = \mu_1 - \mu_2. Since two groups independent, posterior θ\theta distribution difference two independent non-standardised tt-variables: Tj∼tνnj(μnj,σnj†),j=1,2,T_j \;\sim\; t_{\nu_{nj}}(\mu_{nj},\, \sigma_{nj}^{\dagger}), \qquad   j = 1, 2, posterior scale σnj†=σnj/κnj\sigma_{nj}^{\dagger} = \sigma_{nj} / \sqrt{\kappa_{nj}}. posterior probability θ\theta exceeds threshold θ0\theta_0, P(θ>θ0∣𝐲)=P(T1−T2>θ0),P(\theta > \theta_0 \mid \mathbf{y}) =   P(T_1 - T_2 > \theta_0), computed one three methods described Section 3.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: Let ỹ‾j\bar{\tilde{y}}_j denote future sample mean based mjm_j future patients. N-Inv-χ2\chi^2 posterior, predictive distribution ỹ‾j\bar{\tilde{y}}_j non-standardised tt-distribution: ỹ‾j∣𝐲j∼tνnj(μnj,σnj1+κnjκnjmj).\bar{\tilde{y}}_j \mid \mathbf{y}_j \;\sim\;   t_{\nu_{nj}}\!\!\left(\mu_{nj},\;   \sigma_{nj}\sqrt{\frac{1 + \kappa_{nj}}{\kappa_{nj}\, m_j}}\right). vague prior (κnj=nj\kappa_{nj} = n_j, σnj=sj\sigma_{nj} = s_j) becomes ỹ‾j∣𝐲j∼tnj−1(y‾j,sjnj+1njmj).\bar{\tilde{y}}_j \mid \mathbf{y}_j \;\sim\;   t_{n_j - 1}\!\!\left(\bar{y}_j,\;   s_j\sqrt{\frac{n_j + 1}{n_j\, m_j}}\right).

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: posterior predictive probability future treatment effect exceeds null threshold θNULL\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} P(ỹ‾1−ỹ‾2>θNULL∣𝐲),P\!\left(\bar{\tilde{y}}_1 - \bar{\tilde{y}}_2 > \theta_{\mathrm{NULL}}   \mid \mathbf{y}\right), computed three methods posterior probability predictive scale replacing posterior scale.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
Single Continuous Endpoint
text: nMCn_{\mathrm{MC}} independent samples drawn marginal: T1()∼tν1(μ1,σ1),T2()∼tν2(μ2,σ2),=1,…,nMC,T_1^{()} \;\sim\; t_{\nu_1}(\mu_1, \sigma_1), \quad   T_2^{()} \;\sim\; t_{\nu_2}(\mu_2, \sigma_2), \quad   = 1, \ldots, n_{\mathrm{MC}}, probability estimated P̂(D>θ0)=1nMC∑=1nMC𝟏[T1()−T2()>θ0].\widehat{P}(D > \theta_0) =   \frac{1}{n_{\mathrm{MC}}}   \sum_{=1}^{n_{\mathrm{MC}}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[T_1^{()} - T_2^{()} > \theta_0\right]. called pbayesdecisionprob1cont() nsimn_{\mathrm{sim}} simulation replicates, draws generated single nMC×nsimn_{\mathrm{MC}} \times n_{\mathrm{sim}} matrix avoid loop overhead.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
Single Continuous Endpoint
code:
# Observed data (nMC excluded from base list; passed individually per method) args_base <- list(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   theta0 = 1.0,   n1 = 15, n2 = 15,   bar.y1 = 3.2, s1 = 2.0,   bar.y2 = 1.1, s2 = 1.8,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se2 = NULL )  p_ni <- do.call(pbayespostpred1cont,                 c(args_base, list(CalcMethod = 'NI', nMC = NULL))) p_mm <- do.call(pbayespostpred1cont,                 c(args_base, list(CalcMethod = 'MM', nMC = NULL))) set.seed(42) p_mc <- do.call(pbayespostpred1cont,                 c(args_base, list(CalcMethod = 'MC', nMC = 10000L)))  cat(sprintf("NI : %.6f  (exact)
",      p_ni)) #> NI : 0.069397  (exact) cat(sprintf("MM : %.6f  (approx)
",     p_mm)) #> MM : 0.069397  (approx) cat(sprintf("MC : %.6f  (n_MC=10000)
", p_mc)) #> MC : 0.073400  (n_MC=10000)

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: Standard parallel-group RCT observed data treatment (n1n_1, y‾1\bar{y}_1, s1s_1) control (n2n_2, y‾2\bar{y}_2, s2s_2) arms. posteriors updated PoC data. Vague prior — posterior probability: N-Inv-χ2\chi^2 informative prior: Historical knowledge suggests treatment mean ∼3.0\sim 3.0 control mean ∼1.0\sim 1.0 prior pseudo-sample size κ0j=5\kappa_{0j} = 5 degrees freedom ν0j=5\nu_{0j} = 5. Posterior predictive probability (future Phase III: m1=m2=60m_1 = m_2 = 60):
code:
# P(mu1 - mu2 > theta_TV | data) and P(mu1 - mu2 <= theta_MAV | data) p_tv <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'NI', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,   n1 = 15, n2 = 15,   bar.y1 = 3.2, s1 = 2.0,   bar.y2 = 1.1, s2 = 1.8,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE )  p_mav <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'NI', theta0 = 0.5, nMC = NULL,   n1 = 15, n2 = 15,   bar.y1 = 3.2, s1 = 2.0,   bar.y2 = 1.1, s2 = 1.8,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE )  cat(sprintf("P(theta > theta_TV  | data) = %.4f  -> Go  criterion: >= 0.80? %s
",             p_tv, ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80, "YES", "NO"))) #> P(theta > theta_TV  | data) = 0.7940  -> Go  criterion: >= 0.80? NO cat(sprintf("P(theta <= theta_MAV | data) = %.4f  -> NoGo criterion: >= 0.20? %s
",             1 - p_mav, ifelse((1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "YES", "NO"))) #> P(theta <= theta_MAV | data) = 0.0178  -> NoGo criterion: >= 0.20? NO cat(sprintf("Decision: %s
",             ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) < 0.20, "Go",                    ifelse(p_tv < 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "NoGo",                           ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "Miss", "Gray"))))) #> Decision: Gray p_tv_inf <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq',   CalcMethod = 'NI', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,   n1 = 15, n2 = 15,   bar.y1 = 3.2, s1 = 2.0,   bar.y2 = 1.1, s2 = 1.8,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = 5, kappa02 = 5,   nu01    = 5, nu02    = 5,   mu01    = 3.0, mu02  = 1.0,   sigma01 = 2.0, sigma02 = 1.8,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > theta_TV | data, N-Inv-Chisq prior) = %.4f
", p_tv_inf)) #> P(theta > theta_TV | data, N-Inv-Chisq prior) = 0.8274 p_pred <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'NI', theta0 = 1.0, nMC = NULL,   n1 = 15, n2 = 15, m1 = 60, m2 = 60,   bar.y1 = 3.2, s1 = 2.0,   bar.y2 = 1.1, s2 = 1.8,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("Predictive probability (m1 = m2 = 60) = %.4f
", p_pred)) #> Predictive probability (m1 = m2 = 60) = 0.9966

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: concurrent control arm enrolled, control distribution specified external knowledge: Hypothetical control mean: μ02\mu_{02} (fixed scalar) Variance scaling: σcontrol=r⋅σtreatment\sigma_{\mathrm{control}} = \sqrt{r} \cdot \sigma_{\mathrm{treatment}}, r=1r = 1 assumes equal variances posterior control mean updated observed data; treatment posterior updated.
code: p_unctrl <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,   n1 = 15, n2 = 15,   bar.y1 = 3.2, s1 = 2.0,   bar.y2 = NULL, s2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = 1.0,   # hypothetical control mean   sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   r = 1.0,                   # equal variance assumption   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > theta_TV | data, uncontrolled) = %.4f\n", p_unctrl)) #> P(theta > theta_TV | data, uncontrolled) = 0.8184

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: Historical external data prior study incorporated via power prior borrowing weight αe2∈(0,1]\alpha_{e2} \(0, 1]. augmented posterior parameters control group : μn2=αe2ne2y‾e2+n2y‾2αe2ne2+n2,\mu_{n2} = \frac{\alpha_{e2} n_{e2}\, \bar{y}_{e2} + n_2\, \bar{y}_2}   {\alpha_{e2} n_{e2} + n_2}, σn22=αe2(ne2−1)se22+(n2−1)s22+αe2ne2n2(y‾e2−y‾2)2αe2ne2+n2αe2ne2+n2,\sigma_{n2}^2 = \frac{\alpha_{e2}(n_{e2}-1) s_{e2}^2 + (n_2-1) s_2^2 +   \dfrac{\alpha_{e2} n_{e2} n_2 (\bar{y}_{e2} - \bar{y}_2)^2}         {\alpha_{e2} n_{e2} + n_2}}   {\alpha_{e2} n_{e2} + n_2}, degrees freedom νn2=αe2ne2+n2−1\nu_{n2} = \alpha_{e2} n_{e2} + n_2 - 1. , ne2=20n_{e2} = 20, y‾e2=0.9\bar{y}_{e2} = 0.9, se2=1.8s_{e2} = 1.8, αe2=0.5\alpha_{e2} = 0.5 (50% borrowing external control): Effect borrowing weight: varying αe2\alpha_{e2} 0 1 shows external data influence posterior.
code: p_ext <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,   n1 = 15, n2 = 15,   bar.y1 = 3.2, s1 = 2.0,   bar.y2 = 1.1, s2 = 1.8,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = 20L, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = 0.5,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = 0.9, se2 = 1.8,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > theta_TV | data, external control, alpha=0.5) = %.4f\n",             p_ext)) #> P(theta > theta_TV | data, external control, alpha=0.5) = 0.8517 # alpha02 must be in (0, 1]; start from 0.01 to avoid validation error alpha_seq <- c(0.01, seq(0.1, 1.0, by = 0.1)) p_alpha   <- sapply(alpha_seq, function(a) {   pbayespostpred1cont(     prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', prior = 'vague',     CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,     n1 = 15, n2 = 15,     bar.y1 = 3.2, s1 = 2.0,     bar.y2 = 1.1, s2 = 1.8,     m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,     kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,     mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,     r = NULL,     ne1 = NULL, ne2 = 20L, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = a,     bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = 0.9, se2 = 1.8,     lower.tail = FALSE   ) }) data.frame(alpha_e2 = alpha_seq, P_gt_TV = round(p_alpha, 4)) #>    alpha_e2 P_gt_TV #> 1      0.01  0.7994 #> 2      0.10  0.8133 #> 3      0.20  0.8259 #> 4      0.30  0.8361 #> 5      0.40  0.8446 #> 6      0.50  0.8517 #> 7      0.60  0.8577 #> 8      0.70  0.8629 #> 9      0.80  0.8674 #> 10     0.90  0.8713 #> 11     1.00  0.8748

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: given true parameter (μ1,μ2,σ1,σ2)(\mu_1, \mu_2, \sigma_1, \sigma_2), operating characteristics probabilities decision outcome Go/NoGo/Gray/Miss rule thresholds (γ1,γ2)(\gamma_1, \gamma_2). estimated Monte Carlo simulation nsimn_{\mathrm{sim}} replicates: Pr̂(Go)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[gGo()≥γ1 gNoGo()<γ2],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Go}) =   \frac{1}{n_{\mathrm{sim}}} \sum_{=1}^{n_{\mathrm{sim}}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{Go}}^{()} \ge \gamma_1     \;\text{ }\; g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}^{()} < \gamma_2\right], Pr̂(NoGo)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[gGo()<γ1 gNoGo()≥γ2],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{NoGo}) =   \frac{1}{n_{\mathrm{sim}}} \sum_{=1}^{n_{\mathrm{sim}}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{Go}}^{()} < \gamma_1     \;\text{ }\; g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}^{()} \ge \gamma_2\right], Pr̂(Gray)=1−Pr̂(Go)−Pr̂(NoGo)−Pr̂(Miss),\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Gray}) = 1 -   \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Go}) - \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{NoGo}) -   \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Miss}), gGo()=P(θ>θTV∣𝐲()),gNoGo()=P(θ≤θMAV∣𝐲())g_{\mathrm{Go}}^{()} = P(\theta > \theta_{\mathrm{TV}} \mid \mathbf{y}^{()}), \qquad g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}^{()} = P(\theta \le \theta_{\mathrm{MAV}} \mid \mathbf{y}^{()}) evaluated ii-th simulated dataset 𝐲()\mathbf{y}^{()}. Miss (gGo≥γ1g_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_1 gNoGo≥γ2g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_2) indicates inconsistent threshold configuration triggers error default (error_if_Miss = TRUE).

code: oc_ctrl <- pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim       = 500L,   prob       = 'posterior',   design     = 'controlled',   prior      = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM',   theta.TV   = 1.5,  theta.MAV = 0.5,  theta.NULL = NULL,   nMC        = NULL,   gamma1     = 0.80, gamma2    = 0.20,   n1 = 15,   n2 = 15,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   mu1    = c(1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 3.5),   mu2    = 1.0,   sigma1 = 2.0,   sigma2 = 2.0,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE,   seed = 42L ) print(oc_ctrl) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior #>   Design           : controlled #>   Method           : Moment-Matching (MM) #>   Simulations      : 500 #>   Seed             : 42 #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 1.5, MAV = 0.5 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.2 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 15, n2 = 15 #>   True parameters  : sigma1 = 2, sigma2 = 2 #>   Prior (vague)    : (no hyperparameters) #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu1 mu2     Go   Gray   NoGo #>  1.0   1 0.0020 0.0640 0.9340 #>  1.5   1 0.0140 0.1680 0.8180 #>  2.0   1 0.0660 0.3520 0.5820 #>  2.5   1 0.1820 0.5020 0.3160 #>  3.0   1 0.4180 0.4600 0.1220 #>  3.5   1 0.6840 0.2880 0.0280 #> ------------------------------------------------------------

what: 5.3 Figure: Go/NoGo/Gray Probabilities vs. True Treatment Effect
title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: Operating characteristics (Go/NoGo/Gray) function true treatment effect (mu1 - mu2). Controlled design, vague prior, MM method. True mu2 = 1.0, sigma1 = sigma2 = 2.0, n1 = n2 = 15, gamma1 = 0.80, gamma2 = 0.20.
code: # Compute OC over a finer grid of true treatment means mu1_seq <- seq(1.0, 4.5, by = 0.5)  oc_grid <- pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim       = 500L,   prob       = 'posterior',   design     = 'controlled',   prior      = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM',   theta.TV   = 1.5,  theta.MAV = 0.5,  theta.NULL = NULL,   nMC        = NULL,   gamma1     = 0.80, gamma2    = 0.20,   n1 = 15,   n2 = 15,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   mu1    = mu1_seq,   mu2    = 1.0,   sigma1 = 2.0,   sigma2 = 2.0,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE,   seed = 42L )  delta   <- oc_grid$mu1 - 1.0  # true treatment effect (mu1 - mu2)  # --- Plot --- old_par <- par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 1))  plot(delta, oc_grid$Go,      type = "b", pch = 16, lwd = 2,      col  = "#2166AC",      ylim = c(0, 1), xlab = expression(mu[1] - mu[2]),      ylab = "Probability",      main = "Operating Characteristics: Controlled Design (Posterior Prob.)",      las  = 1) lines(delta, oc_grid$NoGo,       type = "b", pch = 17, lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D") lines(delta, oc_grid$Gray,       type = "b", pch = 15, lwd = 2, col = "#878787")  abline(h  = c(0.20, 0.80), lty = 2, col = "gray50") abline(v  = c(0.5, 1.5),   lty = 3, col = "gray70")  legend("right",        legend = c("Pr(Go)", "Pr(NoGo)", "Pr(Gray)"),        col    = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#878787"),        pch    = c(16, 17, 15), lwd = 2, bty = "n")  text(0.5,  0.05, expression(theta[MAV]), col = "gray40", cex = 0.8) text(1.5,  0.05, expression(theta[TV]),  col = "gray40", cex = 0.8) par(old_par)

code: oc_unctrl <- pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim       = 500L,   prob       = 'posterior',   design     = 'uncontrolled',   prior      = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM',   theta.TV   = 1.5,  theta.MAV = 0.5,  theta.NULL = NULL,   nMC        = NULL,   gamma1     = 0.80, gamma2    = 0.20,   n1 = 15,   n2 = 15,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = 1.0, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   mu1    = c(1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 3.5),   mu2    = 1.0,   sigma1 = 2.0,   sigma2 = 2.0,   r      = 1.0,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, se1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE,   seed = 1L ) print(oc_unctrl) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior #>   Design           : uncontrolled #>   Method           : Moment-Matching (MM) #>   Simulations      : 500 #>   Seed             : 1 #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 1.5, MAV = 0.5 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.2 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 15, n2 = 15 #>   True parameters  : sigma1 = 2 #>   Prior (vague)    : (no hyperparameters) #>   Uncontrolled     : r = 1 #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu1     Go   Gray   NoGo #>  1.5 0.0000 0.0940 0.9060 #>  2.0 0.0160 0.3760 0.6080 #>  2.5 0.0940 0.6380 0.2680 #>  3.0 0.3920 0.5500 0.0580 #>  3.5 0.7320 0.2600 0.0080 #> ------------------------------------------------------------

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: getgamma1cont() function finds optimal pair (γ1*,γ2*)(\gamma_1^*, \gamma_2^*) grid search Γ={γ(1),…,γ(K)}⊂(0,1)\Gamma = \{\gamma^{(1)}, \ldots, \gamma^{(K)}\} \subset (0, 1), : γ1*\gamma_1^*: selected Pr(Go∣γ1*)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_1^*) satisfies criterion target value (e.g., Pr(Go)<0.05\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < 0.05 null scenario μ1=μ2\mu_1 = \mu_2). γ2*\gamma_2^*: selected Pr(NoGo∣γ2*)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma_2^*) satisfies criterion (e.g., Pr(NoGo)<0.20\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < 0.20 null scenario). search uses two-stage strategy: Stage 1 (Simulate ): nsimn_{\mathrm{sim}} datasets generated true parameter distribution probabilities gGo()g_{\mathrm{Go}}^{()} gNoGo()g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}^{()} computed replicate — independently γ\gamma. Stage 2 (Sweep Γ\Gamma): candidate γ∈Γ\gamma \\Gamma, Pr(Go∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma) Pr(NoGo∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma) computed empirical proportions precomputed indicator values without probability evaluations.

code: grid_df <- data.frame(   gamma   = res_gamma$gamma_grid,   PrGo    = res_gamma$PrGo_grid,   PrNoGo  = res_gamma$PrNoGo_grid )  old_par2 <- par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 1))  # Initialize the plot before calling low-level plotting functions (lines, abline, text) plot(grid_df$gamma, grid_df$PrGo,      type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#2166AC",      ylim = c(0, 1),      xlab = expression(gamma),      ylab = "Probability",      main = "Optimal Threshold Search: Pr(Go) and Pr(NoGo) vs. Gamma",      las  = 1)  lines(grid_df$gamma, grid_df$PrNoGo,       lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D")  abline(h = 0.05, lty = 2, col = "#2166AC", lwd = 1.2) abline(h = 0.20, lty = 2, col = "#D6604D", lwd = 1.2)  if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma1)) {   abline(v = res_gamma$gamma1, lty = 2, col = "#2166AC", lwd = 2)   text(res_gamma$gamma1 + 0.02, 0.85,        paste0("gamma1* = ", res_gamma$gamma1),        col = "#2166AC", cex = 0.85, adj = 0) }  if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma2)) {   abline(v = res_gamma$gamma2, lty = 2, col = "#D6604D", lwd = 2)   text(res_gamma$gamma2 - 0.02, 0.70,        paste0("gamma2* = ", res_gamma$gamma2),        col = "#D6604D", cex = 0.85, adj = 1) }  legend("topright",        legend = c("Pr(Go | gamma)", "Pr(NoGo | gamma)",                   "Target Go = 0.05", "Target NoGo = 0.20"),        col    = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#2166AC", "#D6604D"),        lty    = c(1, 1, 2, 2), lwd = c(2, 2, 1.2, 1.2),        bty    = "n", cex = 0.85) par(old_par2)

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: vignette covered single continuous endpoint analysis BayesianQDM: Bayesian model: N-Inv-χ2\chi^2 conjugate posterior (vague informative priors) explicit update formulae κnj\kappa_{nj}, νnj\nu_{nj}, μnj\mu_{nj}, σnj2\sigma_{nj}^2. Posterior predictive distributions: marginal tt-distributions μj\mu_j ỹ‾j\bar{\tilde{y}}_j, explicit scale expressions. Three computation methods: NI (exact), MC (simulation), MM (moment-matching via fourth-moment equation); MM recommended large-scale simulation (νj>4\nu_j > 4 required). Three study designs: controlled (arms observed), uncontrolled (μ02\mu_{02} rr fixed prior knowledge), external control (power prior borrowing weight αej\alpha_{ej}). Operating characteristics: Monte Carlo estimation Pr(Go)\Pr(\mathrm{Go}), Pr(NoGo)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}), Pr(Gray)\Pr(\mathrm{Gray}) across true parameter scenarios. Optimal threshold search: two-stage precompute--sweep strategy getgamma1cont() user-specified criteria selection rules. See vignette("single-binary") analogous binary endpoint analysis.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: extend single-endpoint RA trial bivariate binary design two co-primary binary endpoints: Endpoint 1: ACR20 response (1 = responder, 0 = non-responder) Endpoint 2: DAS28 remission (1 = achieved, 0 = achieved) trial enrolls n1=n2=10n_1 = n_2 = 10 patients per arm 1:1 randomised controlled design. Clinically meaningful thresholds (posterior probability): Null hypothesis thresholds (predictive probability): θNULL,1=0.15\theta_{\mathrm{NULL},1} = 0.15, θNULL,2=0.15\theta_{\mathrm{NULL},2} = 0.15. Decision thresholds: γ1=0.80\gamma_1 = 0.80 (Go), γ2=0.80\gamma_2 = 0.80 (NoGo). Observed data (used Sections 2–4): treatment arm counts four response patterns (Y1,Y2)∈{(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1)}(Y_1, Y_2) \\{(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1)\}: Marginal responder counts: treatment y1,1=7y_{1,1} = 7, y1,2=5y_{1,2} = 5; control y2,1=4y_{2,1} = 4, y2,2=4y_{2,2} = 4.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: given true parameters (𝐩1,𝐩2)(\mathbf{p}_1, \mathbf{p}_2), operating characteristics computed exact enumeration possible multinomial count combinations via two-stage strategy. Stage 1 (precomputation): count combinations 𝐱t,\mathbf{x}_{t,} (KtK_t combinations) 𝐱c,j\mathbf{x}_{c,j} (KcK_c combinations) enumerated allmultinom(). pair (,j)(, j), pbayespostpred2bin() computes region probability vector , yielding ĝGo,ij\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},ij} ĝNoGo,ij\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},ij} independent decision threshold. Stage 2 (weighting): given thresholds (γ1,γ2)(\gamma_1, \gamma_2): Pr(Go)=∑=1Kt∑j=1Kcwij𝟏[ĝGo,ij≥γ1 ĝNoGo,ij<γ2],\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \sum_{=1}^{K_t} \sum_{j=1}^{K_c}   w_{ij}\, \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},ij} \ge \gamma_1   \text{ } \hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},ij} < \gamma_2\right], Pr(NoGo)=∑=1Kt∑j=1Kcwij𝟏[ĝNoGo,ij≥γ2 ĝGo,ij<γ1],\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) = \sum_{=1}^{K_t} \sum_{j=1}^{K_c}   w_{ij}\, \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},ij} \ge \gamma_2   \text{ } \hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},ij} < \gamma_1\right], wij=PMult(𝐱t,;n1,𝐩1)×PMult(𝐱c,j;n2,𝐩2)w_{ij} = P_\mathrm{Mult}(\mathbf{x}_{t,};\, n_1, \mathbf{p}_1) \times P_\mathrm{Mult}(\mathbf{x}_{c,j};\, n_2, \mathbf{p}_2), ĝGo,ij=∑r∈GoRegionsP̂(Rr∣𝐱t,,𝐱c,j),\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},ij}   = \sum_{r \\text{GoRegions}}   \hat{P}(R_r \mid \mathbf{x}_{t,}, \mathbf{x}_{c,j}),ĝNoGo,ij=∑r∈NoGoRegionsP̂(Rr∣𝐱t,,𝐱c,j).\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},ij} = \sum_{r \\text{NoGoRegions}} \hat{P}(R_r \mid \mathbf{x}_{t,}, \mathbf{x}_{c,j}). Miss (ĝGo,ij≥γ1\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},ij} \ge \gamma_1 ĝNoGo,ij≥γ2\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},ij} \ge \gamma_2) indicates inconsistent threshold configuration triggers error default (error_if_Miss = TRUE). Gray comprises remaining outcomes. large n1n_1 n2n_2, method = 'MC' option replaces full enumeration nsimn_\mathrm{sim} multinomial draws, deduplicates K≪nsimK \ll n_\mathrm{sim} unique pairs, evaluates pbayespostpred2bin() unique pairs. Note computation time. number multinomial count combinations grows rapidly nn: nkn_k patients 4 response categories number combinations (nk+33)\binom{n_k + 3}{3}, equals 286 nk=10n_k = 10 1771 nk=20n_k = 20. Exact method two arms means 2862≈82,000286^2 \approx 82{,}000 unique pairs requiring nMC Dirichlet draws. predictive probability used (mk>0m_k > 0), additional layer Multinomial sampling added inside Dirichlet draw, multiplying computation time. Use small nMC (100–500) n1 = n2 = 10 demonstration; switch method = 'MC' moderate nsim larger sample sizes.

code: pi_t_seq <- seq(0.15, 0.65, by = 0.05) n_scen   <- length(pi_t_seq)  oc_grid <- pbayesdecisionprob2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   gamma1 = 0.80, gamma2 = 0.80,   pi_t1 = pi_t_seq, pi_t2 = pi_t_seq,   rho_t = rep(0.0, n_scen),   pi_c1 = rep(0.15, n_scen),   pi_c2 = rep(0.15, n_scen),   rho_c = rep(0.0,  n_scen),   n1 = 10L, n2 = 10L,   a1_00 = 0.25, a1_01 = 0.25, a1_10 = 0.25, a1_11 = 0.25,   a2_00 = 0.25, a2_01 = 0.25, a2_10 = 0.25, a2_11 = 0.25,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   theta.TV1  = 0.20, theta.MAV1 = 0.10,   theta.TV2  = 0.20, theta.MAV2 = 0.10,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,   xe1_00 = NULL, xe1_01 = NULL, xe1_10 = NULL, xe1_11 = NULL,   xe2_00 = NULL, xe2_01 = NULL, xe2_10 = NULL, xe2_11 = NULL,   ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   nMC = 200L, method = 'Exact',   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE )  delta1 <- oc_grid$pi_t1 - 0.15  old_par <- par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 1))  plot(delta1, oc_grid$Go,      type = "b", pch = 16, lwd = 2, col = "#2166AC",      ylim = c(0, 1),      xlab = expression(pi[t1] - pi[c1]),      ylab = "Probability",      main = "Operating Characteristics: Two Binary Endpoints",      las  = 1) lines(delta1, oc_grid$NoGo,       type = "b", pch = 17, lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D") lines(delta1, oc_grid$Gray,       type = "b", pch = 15, lwd = 2, col = "#878787")  abline(h = c(0.20, 0.80), lty = 2, col = "gray50") abline(v = c(0.10, 0.20), lty = 3, col = "gray70")  text(0.10 + 0.005, 0.04,      expression(theta[MAV1]), col = "gray40", cex = 0.8, adj = 0) text(0.20 + 0.005, 0.04,      expression(theta[TV1]),  col = "gray40", cex = 0.8, adj = 0)  legend("right",        legend = c("Pr(Go)", "Pr(NoGo)", "Pr(Gray)"),        col    = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#878787"),        pch    = c(16, 17, 15), lwd = 2, bty = "n") par(old_par)

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: getgamma2bin() finds optimal pair (γ1*,γ2*)(\gamma_1^*, \gamma_2^*) two-stage precompute--sweep strategy pbayesdecisionprob2bin(), sweeps two-dimensional grid Γ1×Γ2\Gamma_1 \times \Gamma_2. Stage 1 (precomputation): pbayespostpred2bin() called every unique multinomial outcome combination (xt,,xc,j)(x_{t,}, x_{c,j}) enumerated allmultinom(). region probability vector summed GoRegions NoGoRegions obtain ĝGo,ij\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},ij} ĝNoGo,ij\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},ij}, independent (γ1,γ2)(\gamma_1, \gamma_2). Stage 2 (gamma sweep): every pair (γ1,γ2)∈Γ1×Γ2(\gamma_1, \gamma_2) \\Gamma_1 \times \Gamma_2: Pr(Go∣γ1,γ2)=∑,jwij𝟏[ĝGo,ij≥γ1 ĝNoGo,ij<γ2],\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_1, \gamma_2) =   \sum_{,j} w_{ij}\,   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},ij} \ge \gamma_1 \text{ }   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},ij} < \gamma_2\right], Pr(NoGo∣γ1,γ2)=∑,jwij𝟏[ĝNoGo,ij≥γ2 ĝGo,ij<γ1].\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma_1, \gamma_2) =   \sum_{,j} w_{ij}\,   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},ij} \ge \gamma_2 \text{ }   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},ij} < \gamma_1\right]. Results stored |Γ1|×|Γ2||\Gamma_1| \times |\Gamma_2| matrices PrGo_grid PrNoGo_grid. Stage 3 (optimal selection): candidate γ1\gamma_1, worst-case Pr(Go)\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) γ2\gamma_2 compared target_go; analogously γ2\gamma_2.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: Null scenario: πt1=πc1=0.15\pi_{t1} = \pi_{c1} = 0.15, πt2=πc2=0.15\pi_{t2} = \pi_{c2} = 0.15, ρt=ρc=0\rho_t = \rho_c = 0 (treatment effect, independence).
code: res_gamma <- getgamma2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   pi_t1 = 0.15, pi_t2 = 0.15, rho_t = 0.0,   pi_c1 = 0.15, pi_c2 = 0.15, rho_c = 0.0,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go   = '<',  crit_nogo   = '<',   sel_go    = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 10L, n2 = 10L,   a1_00 = 0.25, a1_01 = 0.25, a1_10 = 0.25, a1_11 = 0.25,   a2_00 = 0.25, a2_01 = 0.25, a2_10 = 0.25, a2_11 = 0.25,   theta.TV1  = 0.20, theta.MAV1 = 0.10,   theta.TV2  = 0.20, theta.MAV2 = 0.10,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,   xe1_00 = NULL, xe1_01 = NULL, xe1_10 = NULL, xe1_11 = NULL,   xe2_00 = NULL, xe2_01 = NULL, xe2_10 = NULL, xe2_11 = NULL,   ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   nMC = 200L, method = 'Exact',   gamma1_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05) ) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma1* = %.2f  (Pr(Go) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma$gamma1, res_gamma$PrGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma1* = 0.15  (Pr(Go) = 0.0446) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma2* = %.2f  (Pr(NoGo) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma$gamma2, res_gamma$PrNoGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma2* = 0.95  (Pr(NoGo) = 0.0245)

what: 5.3 Figure: Contour Plot of Pr(Go) over (γ1,γ2)(\gamma_1, \gamma_2) Grid
title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: Contour plot Pr(Go) function candidate threshold pair (gamma1, gamma2) null scenario (pi_t = pi_c = (0.15, 0.15), rho = 0). 0.05 target contour highlighted bold (blue). Dashed lines mark optimal gamma1* (blue) gamma2* (red).
code: g1   <- res_gamma$gamma1_grid g2   <- res_gamma$gamma2_grid Z_go <- res_gamma$PrGo_grid   # rows = gamma1, cols = gamma2  go_upper  <- ceiling(quantile(Z_go, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE) * 20) / 20 go_upper  <- max(go_upper, 0.10) go_breaks <- seq(0, go_upper, length.out = 11) go_cols   <- hcl.colors(10, palette = "Blues", rev = TRUE)  layout(matrix(1:2, nrow = 1), widths = c(8, 2)) par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3.5, 1))  image(x = g1, y = g2, z = pmin(Z_go, go_upper),       breaks = go_breaks, col = go_cols,       xlab = expression(gamma[1]),       ylab = expression(gamma[2]),       main = expression("Pr(Go) over (" * gamma[1] * ", " * gamma[2] * ") grid"),       las  = 1,       useRaster = TRUE) contour(x = g1, y = g2, z = Z_go,         levels = 0.05, add = TRUE,         lwd = 2.5, col = "#2166AC", labcex = 0.9) if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma1))   abline(v = res_gamma$gamma1, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#2166AC") if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma2))   abline(h = res_gamma$gamma2, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D") legend("topright",        legend = c(          paste0("gamma1* = ", res_gamma$gamma1),          paste0("gamma2* = ", res_gamma$gamma2),          "Pr(Go) = 0.05 contour"),        col = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#2166AC"),        lty = c(2, 2, 1), lwd = c(2, 2, 2.5),        bty = "n", cex = 0.85)  # Color bar par(mar = c(4.5, 0.5, 3.5, 3))  plot(NULL, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, go_upper), type = "n",       axes = FALSE, xlab = "", ylab = "") rect(0, go_breaks[-11], 1, go_breaks[-1],       col = go_cols,       border = NA) axis(4, at = seq(0, go_upper, length.out = 6), las = 1, cex.axis = 0.8) layout(1) par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)

what: 5.4 Figure: Contour Plot of Pr(NoGo) over (γ1,γ2)(\gamma_1, \gamma_2) Grid
title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: Contour plot Pr(NoGo) function (gamma1, gamma2) null scenario. 0.20 target contour highlighted bold (red). Dashed lines mark optimal thresholds.
code: Z_nogo <- res_gamma$PrNoGo_grid  nogo_upper  <- ceiling(quantile(Z_nogo, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE) * 20) / 20 nogo_upper  <- max(nogo_upper, 0.30) nogo_breaks <- seq(0, nogo_upper, length.out = 11) nogo_cols   <- hcl.colors(10, palette = "Reds", rev = TRUE)  layout(matrix(1:2, nrow = 1), widths = c(8, 2)) par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3.5, 1))  image(x = g1, y = g2, z = pmin(Z_nogo, nogo_upper),       breaks = nogo_breaks, col = nogo_cols,       xlab = expression(gamma[1]),       ylab = expression(gamma[2]),       main = expression("Pr(NoGo) over (" * gamma[1] * ", " * gamma[2] * ") grid \u2014 Null scenario"),       las  = 1) contour(x = g1, y = g2, z = Z_nogo,         levels = 0.20, add = TRUE,         lwd = 2.5, col = "#D6604D", labcex = 0.9) if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma1))   abline(v = res_gamma$gamma1, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#2166AC") if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma2))   abline(h = res_gamma$gamma2, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D") legend("topright",        legend = c(          paste0("gamma1* = ", res_gamma$gamma1),          paste0("gamma2* = ", res_gamma$gamma2),          "Pr(NoGo) = 0.20 contour"),        col = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#D6604D"),        lty = c(2, 2, 1), lwd = c(2, 2, 2.5),        bty = "n", cex = 0.85)  # Color bar par(mar = c(4.5, 0.5, 3.5, 3))  plot(NULL, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, nogo_upper), type = "n",       axes = FALSE, xlab = "", ylab = "") rect(0, nogo_breaks[-11], 1, nogo_breaks[-1],       col = nogo_cols,       border = NA) axis(4, at = seq(0, nogo_upper, length.out = 6), las = 1, cex.axis = 0.8) layout(1) par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)

code: res_gamma_unctrl <- getgamma2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   pi_t1 = 0.15, pi_t2 = 0.15, rho_t = 0.0,   pi_c1 = NULL, pi_c2 = NULL, rho_c = NULL,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go   = '<',  crit_nogo   = '<',   sel_go    = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 10L, n2 = 10L,   a1_00 = 0.25, a1_01 = 0.25, a1_10 = 0.25, a1_11 = 0.25,   a2_00 = 0.25, a2_01 = 0.25, a2_10 = 0.25, a2_11 = 0.25,   theta.TV1  = 0.20, theta.MAV1 = 0.10,   theta.TV2  = 0.20, theta.MAV2 = 0.10,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   z00 = 3L, z01 = 2L, z10 = 2L, z11 = 1L,   xe1_00 = NULL, xe1_01 = NULL, xe1_10 = NULL, xe1_11 = NULL,   xe2_00 = NULL, xe2_01 = NULL, xe2_10 = NULL, xe2_11 = NULL,   ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   nMC = 200L, method = 'Exact',   gamma1_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05) ) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma1* = %.2f  (Pr(Go) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma_unctrl$gamma1, res_gamma_unctrl$PrGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma1* = 0.05  (Pr(Go) = 0.0038) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma2* = %.2f  (Pr(NoGo) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma_unctrl$gamma2, res_gamma_unctrl$PrNoGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma2* = 0.95  (Pr(NoGo) = 0.1876)

text: Note. prob = 'predictive', getgamma2bin() must evaluate pbayespostpred2bin() every outcome combination (xt,,xc,j)(x_{t,}, x_{c,j}) using nMC Dirichlet draws mkm_k future observations . substantially increases computation time relative posterior case. method = 'MC' option moderate nsim small nMC recommended vignette-level demonstration.
code: res_gamma_pred <- getgamma2bin(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 4L,   pi_t1 = 0.15, pi_t2 = 0.15, rho_t = 0.0,   pi_c1 = 0.15, pi_c2 = 0.15, rho_c = 0.0,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go   = '<',  crit_nogo   = '<',   sel_go    = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 10L, n2 = 10L,   a1_00 = 0.25, a1_01 = 0.25, a1_10 = 0.25, a1_11 = 0.25,   a2_00 = 0.25, a2_01 = 0.25, a2_10 = 0.25, a2_11 = 0.25,   theta.TV1  = NULL, theta.MAV1 = NULL,   theta.TV2  = NULL, theta.MAV2 = NULL,   theta.NULL1 = 0.15, theta.NULL2 = 0.15,   m1 = 15L, m2 = 15L,   z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,   xe1_00 = NULL, xe1_01 = NULL, xe1_10 = NULL, xe1_11 = NULL,   xe2_00 = NULL, xe2_01 = NULL, xe2_10 = NULL, xe2_11 = NULL,   ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   nMC = 100L, method = 'MC', nsim = 1000L,   gamma1_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05) ) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma1* = %.2f  (Pr(Go) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma_pred$gamma1, res_gamma_pred$PrGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma1* = 0.25  (Pr(Go) = 0.0373) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma2* = %.2f  (Pr(NoGo) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma_pred$gamma2, res_gamma_pred$PrNoGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma2* = 0.95  (Pr(NoGo) = 0.0323)

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: vignette covered two binary endpoint analysis BayesianQDM: Bayesian model: Dirichlet-Multinomial conjugate, modelling bivariate binary outcome four-cell probability vector 𝐩k\mathbf{p}_k. Posterior update: αk,lm*=αk,lm+xk,lm\alpha_{k,lm}^* = \alpha_{k,lm} + x_{k,lm}. symmetric Jeffreys-type prior αk,lm=0.25\alpha_{k,lm} = 0.25 recommended. Within-arm correlation: parameterised via ρk\rho_k using moment-matching identity; feasible range enforced getjointbin(). Nine-region grid posterior probability four-region grid predictive probability, identical structure two continuous endpoint case. Monte Carlo evaluation region probabilities via Dirichlet draws; closed-form expression joint probability exists bivariate binary case. Three study designs: controlled (standard posterior update), uncontrolled (hypothetical count vector 𝐳\mathbf{z} fixes control Dirichlet distribution), external (Dirichlet power prior αk,lm*=αk,lm+a0kxek,lm\alpha_{k,lm}^* = \alpha_{k,lm} + a_{0k}\, x_{ek,lm}). Exact operating characteristics: two-stage precomputation multinomial count combinations via allmultinom(), multinomial probability weighting (outer Monte Carlo needed small nn). Optimal threshold search: three-stage precompute--sweep getgamma2bin(), producing |Γ1|×|Γ2||\Gamma_1| \times |\Gamma_2| matrices PrGo_grid PrNoGo_grid, visualised contour plots. See vignette("overview") comparison across four endpoint types.

title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: extend single-endpoint RA trial bivariate design two co-primary continuous endpoints: Endpoint 1: change baseline DAS28 score Endpoint 2: change baseline HAQ-DI (Health Assessment Questionnaire – Disability Index) trial enrolls n1=n2=20n_1 = n_2 = 20 patients per arm (treatment vs. placebo) 1:1 randomised controlled design. Clinically meaningful thresholds (posterior probability): Null hypothesis thresholds (predictive probability): θNULL,1=0.5\theta_{\mathrm{NULL},1} = 0.5, θNULL,2=0.3\theta_{\mathrm{NULL},2} = 0.3. Decision thresholds: γ1=0.80\gamma_1 = 0.80 (Go), γ2=0.20\gamma_2 = 0.20 (NoGo). Observed data (used Sections 2–4): Treatment: 𝐲‾1=(3.5,2.1)⊤\bar{\mathbf{y}}_1 = (3.5, 2.1)^\top, S1=(18.03.63.69.0)S_1 = \begin{pmatrix}18.0 & 3.6\\3.6 & 9.0\end{pmatrix} Control: 𝐲‾2=(1.8,1.0)⊤\bar{\mathbf{y}}_2 = (1.8, 1.0)^\top, S2=(16.02.82.88.5)S_2 = \begin{pmatrix}16.0 & 2.8\\2.8 & 8.5\end{pmatrix} Sk=∑=1nk(𝐲ki−𝐲‾k)(𝐲ki−𝐲‾k)⊤S_k = \sum_{=1}^{n_k}(\mathbf{y}_{ki} - \bar{\mathbf{y}}_k) (\mathbf{y}_{ki} - \bar{\mathbf{y}}_k)^\top sum--squares matrix.

S1 <- matrix(c(18.0, 3.6, 3.6, 9.0), 2, 2) S2 <- matrix(c(16.0, 2.8, 2.8, 8.5), 2, 2)  set.seed(42) p_post_vague <- pbayespostpred2cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   theta.TV1  = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,   theta.TV2  = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L,   ybar1 = c(3.5, 2.1), S1 = S1,   ybar2 = c(1.8, 1.0), S2 = S2,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,   kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = 5000L ) print(round(p_post_vague, 4)) #>     R1     R2     R3     R4     R5     R6     R7     R8     R9  #> 0.5056 0.2198 0.0000 0.1478 0.1264 0.0002 0.0000 0.0002 0.0000 cat(sprintf(   "g_Go  = P(R1 | data) = %.4f
", p_post_vague["R1"] )) #> g_Go  = P(R1 | data) = 0.5056 cat(sprintf(   "g_NoGo = P(R9 | data) = %.4f

", p_post_vague["R9"] )) #> g_NoGo = P(R9 | data) = 0.0000 cat(sprintf(   "Go  criterion: g_Go  >= gamma1 (0.80)? %s
",   ifelse(p_post_vague["R1"] >= 0.80, "YES", "NO") )) #> Go  criterion: g_Go  >= gamma1 (0.80)? NO cat(sprintf(   "NoGo criterion: g_NoGo >= gamma2 (0.20)? %s
",   ifelse(p_post_vague["R9"] >= 0.20, "YES", "NO") )) #> NoGo criterion: g_NoGo >= gamma2 (0.20)? NO cat(sprintf("Decision: %s
",   ifelse(p_post_vague["R1"] >= 0.80 & p_post_vague["R9"] <  0.20, "Go",   ifelse(p_post_vague["R1"] <  0.80 & p_post_vague["R9"] >= 0.20, "NoGo",   ifelse(p_post_vague["R1"] >= 0.80 & p_post_vague["R9"] >= 0.20, "Miss",                                                                     "Gray"))) )) #> Decision: Gray L0 <- matrix(c(8.0, 0.0, 0.0, 2.0), 2, 2)  set.seed(42) p_post_niw <- pbayespostpred2cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',   theta.TV1  = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,   theta.TV2  = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L,   ybar1 = c(3.5, 2.1), S1 = S1,   ybar2 = c(1.8, 1.0), S2 = S2,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = 2.0, nu01 = 5.0, mu01 = c(2.0, 1.0), Lambda01 = L0,   kappa02 = 2.0, nu02 = 5.0, mu02 = c(0.0, 0.0), Lambda02 = L0,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = 5000L ) print(round(p_post_niw, 4)) #>     R1     R2     R3     R4     R5     R6     R7     R8     R9  #> 0.5116 0.2228 0.0000 0.1320 0.1324 0.0010 0.0002 0.0000 0.0000 set.seed(42) p_pred <- pbayespostpred2cont(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   theta.TV1 = NULL, theta.MAV1 = NULL,   theta.TV2 = NULL, theta.MAV2 = NULL,   theta.NULL1 = 0.5, theta.NULL2 = 0.3,   n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L,   ybar1 = c(3.5, 2.1), S1 = S1,   ybar2 = c(1.8, 1.0), S2 = S2,   m1 = 60L, m2 = 60L,   kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,   kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = 5000L ) print(round(p_pred, 4)) #> R1 R2 R3 R4  #>  1  0  0  0 cat(sprintf(   "
Go  region (R1): P = %.4f  >= gamma1 (0.80)? %s
",   p_pred["R1"], ifelse(p_pred["R1"] >= 0.80, "YES", "NO") )) #>  #> Go  region (R1): P = 1.0000  >= gamma1 (0.80)? YES cat(sprintf(   "NoGo region (R4): P = %.4f  >= gamma2 (0.20)? %s
",   p_pred["R4"], ifelse(p_pred["R4"] >= 0.20, "YES", "NO") )) #> NoGo region (R4): P = 0.0000  >= gamma2 (0.20)? NO cat(sprintf("Decision: %s
",   ifelse(p_pred["R1"] >= 0.80 & p_pred["R4"] <  0.20, "Go",   ifelse(p_pred["R1"] <  0.80 & p_pred["R4"] >= 0.20, "NoGo",   ifelse(p_pred["R1"] >= 0.80 & p_pred["R4"] >= 0.20, "Miss",                                                         "Gray"))) )) #> Decision: Go

text: given true parameters (𝛍1,Σ1,𝛍2,Σ2)(\boldsymbol{\mu}_1, \Sigma_1, \boldsymbol{\mu}_2, \Sigma_2), operating characteristics estimated Monte Carlo nsimn_\mathrm{sim} replicates. Let ĝGo,=P(GoRegions∣𝐘())\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} = P(\text{GoRegions} \mid \mathbf{Y}^{()}) ĝNoGo,=P(NoGoRegions∣𝐘())\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} = P(\text{NoGoRegions} \mid \mathbf{Y}^{()}) ii-th simulated dataset. : Pr̂(Go)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝGo,≥γ1andĝNoGo,<γ2],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Go}) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} \ge \gamma_1 \;\text{}\;   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} < \gamma_2\right], Pr̂(NoGo)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝNoGo,≥γ2andĝGo,<γ1],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{NoGo}) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} \ge \gamma_2 \;\text{}\;   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} < \gamma_1\right], Pr̂(Miss)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝGo,≥γ1andĝNoGo,≥γ2],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Miss}) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} \ge \gamma_1 \;\text{}\;   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} \ge \gamma_2\right], Pr̂(Gray)=1−Pr̂(Go)−Pr̂(NoGo)−Pr̂(Miss).\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Gray}) = 1 -   \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Go}) - \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{NoGo}) -   \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Miss}). Miss (ĝGo≥γ1\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_1 ĝNoGo≥γ2\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_2 simultaneously) indicates inconsistent threshold configuration triggers error default (error_if_Miss = TRUE).
