title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: Operating characteristics plotted function treatment effect Endpoint 1 (μ1,1−μ2,1\mu_{1,1} - \mu_{2,1}). endpoints increase together (μ1,2=0.6×μ1,1\mu_{1,2} = 0.6 \times \mu_{1,1}) show transition NoGo Gray Go clearly. Operating characteristics (Go/NoGo/Gray) function true treatment effect Endpoint 1. endpoints increase proportionally (mu1_2 = 0.6 * mu1_1). Controlled design, vague prior, MC method. n1 = n2 = 20, gamma1 = 0.80, gamma2 = 0.20.
code: delta1_seq <- seq(0.0, 3.5, by = 0.25)  oc_grid <- pbayesdecisionprob2cont(   nsim = 300L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   prior = 'vague',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   gamma1 = 0.80, gamma2 = 0.20,   theta.TV1  = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,   theta.TV2  = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   mu1    = cbind(delta1_seq, 0.6 * delta1_seq),   Sigma1 = Sigma,   mu2    = matrix(0, nrow = length(delta1_seq), ncol = 2),   Sigma2 = Sigma,   kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,   kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 42L )  old_par <- par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 1))  plot(delta1_seq, oc_grid$Go,      type = "b", pch = 16, lwd = 2, col = "#2166AC",      ylim = c(0, 1),      xlab = expression(mu[paste("1,1")] - mu[paste("2,1")]),      ylab = "Probability",      main = "Operating Characteristics: Two Continuous Endpoints",      las  = 1) lines(delta1_seq, oc_grid$NoGo,       type = "b", pch = 17, lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D") lines(delta1_seq, oc_grid$Gray,       type = "b", pch = 15, lwd = 2, col = "#878787")  abline(h = c(0.20, 0.80), lty = 2, col = "gray50") abline(v = c(0.5, 1.5),   lty = 3, col = "gray70")  text(0.5 + 0.05, 0.04, expression(theta[MAV1]), col = "gray40", cex = 0.8, adj = 0) text(1.5 + 0.05, 0.04, expression(theta[TV1]),  col = "gray40", cex = 0.8, adj = 0)  legend("right",        legend = c("Pr(Go)", "Pr(NoGo)", "Pr(Gray)"),        col    = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#878787"),        pch    = c(16, 17, 15), lwd = 2, bty = "n") par(old_par)

title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: two decision thresholds (γ1,γ2)(\gamma_1, \gamma_2), optimal search performed two-dimensional grid Γ1×Γ2\Gamma_1 \times \Gamma_2. getgamma2cont() uses three-stage strategy: Stage 1 (Simulate precompute): nsimn_\mathrm{sim} bivariate datasets generated null scenario (𝛍10,𝛍20,Σ1,Σ2)(\boldsymbol{\mu}_1^0, \boldsymbol{\mu}_2^0, \Sigma_1, \Sigma_2). replicate ii, pbayespostpred2cont() called vectorised mode return full region probability vector. Go NoGo probabilities per replicate : ĝGo,=∑j∈GoRegionsp̂ij,ĝNoGo,=∑j∈NoGoRegionsp̂ij.\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} = \sum_{j \\text{GoRegions}} \hat{p}_{ij}, \qquad   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} = \sum_{j \\text{NoGoRegions}} \hat{p}_{ij}. Stage 2 (Gamma sweep): every pair (γ1,γ2)∈Γ1×Γ2(\gamma_1, \gamma_2) \\Gamma_1 \times \Gamma_2: Pr(Go∣γ1,γ2)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝGo,≥γ1 ĝNoGo,<γ2],\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_1, \gamma_2) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} \ge \gamma_1 \text{ }   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} < \gamma_2\right], Pr(NoGo∣γ1,γ2)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝNoGo,≥γ2 ĝGo,<γ1].\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma_1, \gamma_2) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} \ge \gamma_2 \text{ }   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} < \gamma_1\right]. results stored |Γ1|×|Γ2||\Gamma_1| \times |\Gamma_2| matrices PrGo_grid PrNoGo_grid — ideal contour visualisation. Stage 3 (Optimal selection): candidate γ1\gamma_1, worst-case (maximum) Pr(Go)\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) γ2\gamma_2 compared target_go using crit_go; analogously γ2\gamma_2.

title: Two Continuous Endpoints
code: res_gamma <- getgamma2cont(   nsim = 500L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   prior = 'vague',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   mu1 = c(0.5, 0.3), Sigma1 = Sigma,   # null: effect at MAV boundary   mu2 = c(0.0, 0.0), Sigma2 = Sigma,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',   sel_go  = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L,   theta.TV1  = 1.5, theta.MAV1 = 0.5,   theta.TV2  = 1.0, theta.MAV2 = 0.3,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,   kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',   gamma1_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   seed = 42L ) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma1* = %.2f  (Pr(Go) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma$gamma1, res_gamma$PrGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma1* = 0.15  (Pr(Go) = 0.0400) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma2* = %.2f  (Pr(NoGo) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma$gamma2, res_gamma$PrNoGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma2* = 0.95  (Pr(NoGo) = 0.0040)

what: 6.3 Figure: Contour Plot of Pr(Go) over (γ1,γ2)(\gamma_1, \gamma_2) Grid
title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: Contour plot Pr(Go) function candidate threshold pair (gamma1, gamma2) null scenario (mu1 = (0.5, 0.3), mu2 = (0, 0)). dashed lines mark optimal gamma1* (blue) gamma2* (red). 0.05 contour highlighted bold.
code: g1   <- res_gamma$gamma1_grid g2   <- res_gamma$gamma2_grid Z_go <- res_gamma$PrGo_grid   # rows = gamma1, cols = gamma2  # Cap upper limit at 95th percentile to avoid sparse high-value bands go_upper  <- ceiling(quantile(Z_go, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE) * 20) / 20 go_upper  <- max(go_upper, 0.10) go_breaks <- seq(0, go_upper, length.out = 11) go_cols   <- hcl.colors(10, palette = "Blues", rev = TRUE)  layout(matrix(1:2, nrow = 1), widths = c(8, 2)) par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3.5, 1))  image(x = g1, y = g2, z = pmin(Z_go, go_upper),       breaks = go_breaks, col = go_cols,       xlab = expression(gamma[1]),       ylab = expression(gamma[2]),       main = expression("Pr(Go) over (" * gamma[1] * ", " * gamma[2] * ") grid \u2014 Null scenario"),       las  = 1) contour(x = g1, y = g2, z = Z_go,         levels = 0.05, add = TRUE,         lwd = 2.5, col = "#2166AC", labcex = 0.9) if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma1))   abline(v = res_gamma$gamma1, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#2166AC") if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma2))   abline(h = res_gamma$gamma2, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D") legend("topright",        legend = c(          paste0("gamma1* = ", res_gamma$gamma1),          paste0("gamma2* = ", res_gamma$gamma2),          "Pr(Go) = 0.05 contour"),        col = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#2166AC"),        lty = c(2, 2, 1), lwd = c(2, 2, 2.5),        bty = "n", cex = 0.85)  # Color bar par(mar = c(4.5, 0.5, 3.5, 3))  plot(NULL, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, go_upper), type = "n",       axes = FALSE, xlab = "", ylab = "") rect(0, go_breaks[-11], 1, go_breaks[-1],       col = go_cols,       border = NA) axis(4, at = seq(0, go_upper, length.out = 6), las = 1, cex.axis = 0.8) layout(1) par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)

title: Two Continuous Endpoints

code: Z_nogo <- res_gamma$PrNoGo_grid  nogo_upper  <- ceiling(quantile(Z_nogo, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE) * 20) / 20 nogo_upper  <- max(nogo_upper, 0.30) nogo_breaks <- seq(0, nogo_upper, length.out = 11) nogo_cols   <- hcl.colors(10, palette = "Reds", rev = TRUE)  layout(matrix(1:2, nrow = 1), widths = c(8, 2)) par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3.5, 1))  image(x = g1, y = g2, z = pmin(Z_nogo, nogo_upper),       breaks = nogo_breaks, col = nogo_cols,       xlab = expression(gamma[1]),       ylab = expression(gamma[2]),       main = expression("Pr(NoGo) over (" * gamma[1] * ", " * gamma[2] * ") grid \u2014 Null scenario"),       las  = 1) contour(x = g1, y = g2, z = Z_nogo,         levels = 0.20, add = TRUE,         lwd = 2.5, col = "#D6604D", labcex = 0.9) if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma1))   abline(v = res_gamma$gamma1, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#2166AC") if (!is.na(res_gamma$gamma2))   abline(h = res_gamma$gamma2, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#D6604D") legend("topright",        legend = c(          paste0("gamma1* = ", res_gamma$gamma1),          paste0("gamma2* = ", res_gamma$gamma2),          "Pr(NoGo) = 0.20 contour"),        col = c("#2166AC", "#D6604D", "#D6604D"),        lty = c(2, 2, 1), lwd = c(2, 2, 2.5),        bty = "n", cex = 0.85)  # Color bar par(mar = c(4.5, 0.5, 3.5, 3))  plot(NULL, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, nogo_upper), type = "n",       axes = FALSE, xlab = "", ylab = "") rect(0, nogo_breaks[-11], 1, nogo_breaks[-1],       col = nogo_cols,       border = NA) axis(4, at = seq(0, nogo_upper, length.out = 6), las = 1, cex.axis = 0.8) layout(1) par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)

code: res_gamma_pred <- getgamma2cont(   nsim = 500L, prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled',   prior = 'vague',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 4L,   mu1 = c(0.5, 0.3), Sigma1 = Sigma,   mu2 = c(0.0, 0.0), Sigma2 = Sigma,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',   sel_go  = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 20L, n2 = 20L,   theta.TV1 = NULL, theta.MAV1 = NULL,   theta.TV2 = NULL, theta.MAV2 = NULL,   theta.NULL1 = 0.5, theta.NULL2 = 0.3,   m1 = 60L, m2 = 60L,   kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,   kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',   gamma1_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   seed = 3L ) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma1* = %.2f  (Pr(Go) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma_pred$gamma1, res_gamma_pred$PrGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma1* = NA  (Pr(Go) = NA) cat(sprintf("Optimal gamma2* = %.2f  (Pr(NoGo) = %.4f)\n",             res_gamma_pred$gamma2, res_gamma_pred$PrNoGo_at_gamma)) #> Optimal gamma2* = 0.95  (Pr(NoGo) = NA)

title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: vignette covered two continuous endpoint analysis BayesianQDM: Bayesian model: NIW conjugate posterior explicit update formulae κnk\kappa_{nk}, νnk\nu_{nk}, 𝛍nk\boldsymbol{\mu}_{nk}, Λnk\Lambda_{nk}; bivariate non-standardised tt marginal posterior. Nine-region grid posterior probability four-region grid predictive probability; typical choice Go = R1, NoGo = R9 (R4). Two computation methods: MC (vectorised pre-generated draws Cholesky reuse) MM (fourth-moment matching per component + mvtnorm::pmvt joint probability). Three study designs: controlled, uncontrolled (𝛍0c\boldsymbol{\mu}_{0c} rr fixed), external (NIW power prior explicit augmentation formulae κ0caug\kappa_{0c}^{\mathrm{aug}}, ν0caug\nu_{0c}^{\mathrm{aug}}, 𝛍0caug\boldsymbol{\mu}_{0c}^{\mathrm{aug}}, Λ0caug\Lambda_{0c}^{\mathrm{aug}}). Operating characteristics: Monte Carlo estimation vectorised region probability computation. Optimal threshold search: three-stage strategy getgamma2cont() producing |Γ1|×|Γ2||\Gamma_1| \times |\Gamma_2| matrices PrGo_grid PrNoGo_grid — visualised contour plots target level curves (Pr(Go)<0.05\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < 0.05 null, Pr(NoGo)≥0.20\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) \ge 0.20 null). See vignette("two-binary") analogous two binary endpoint analysis.

title: BayesianQDM
text: BayesianQDM provides comprehensive framework Bayesian Quantitative Decision-Making clinical trials. package enables researchers compute posterior probabilities, posterior predictive probabilities, Go/NoGo/Gray decision probabilities single two-endpoint analyses binary continuous outcomes. package also provides functions find optimal Go/NoGo thresholds satisfy user-specified operating characteristic criteria.

title: BayesianQDM
text: Single Two Endpoints: Supports single binary, single continuous, two binary, two continuous endpoint analyses Multiple Study Designs: Controlled, uncontrolled (hypothetical control), external control designs Flexible Prior Specification: Vague (Jeffreys) priors conjugate priors (Beta, Normal-Inverse-Chi-squared, Normal-Inverse-Wishart, Dirichlet) Power Priors: Exact conjugate representation incorporating historical external data without MCMC Multiple Calculation Methods: Numerical integration (NI), Monte Carlo simulation (MC), Moment-Matching approximation (MM) continuous endpoints Three-Zone Decision Framework: Go/NoGo/Gray probability calculation user-defined thresholds (TV MAV) Optimal Threshold Search: Grid-search functions find Go/NoGo thresholds satisfying target operating characteristics

title: BayesianQDM
code: install.packages("BayesianQDM")

title: BayesianQDM
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("gosukehommaEX/BayesianQDM")

title: BayesianQDM
text: Standard randomised controlled trials concurrent treatment control groups.

title: BayesianQDM
text: Single-arm studies using hypothetical control distribution specified prior parameters variance scaling factor.

title: BayesianQDM
text: Incorporates historical external control (/treatment) data power priors using exact conjugate representations, enabling efficient Bayesian computation without MCMC sampling.

title: BayesianQDM
text: single continuous endpoints, three methods available computing CDF difference two independent t-distributions: NI (Numerical Integration): Exact computation via adaptive quadrature (stats::integrate) MC (Monte Carlo): Simulation-based estimation; supports vectorised batch processing MM (Moment-Matching): Closed-form approximation matching first two even moments difference distribution single t-distribution; fully vectorised recommended large-scale simulation studies two continuous endpoints, MC MM methods available computing joint rectangular region probabilities bivariate t-distribution approximation (Theorem 3, Homma Yamaguchi, 2025).

title: BayesianQDM
text: getgamma family functions finds optimal Go threshold γ1\gamma_1 NoGo threshold γ2\gamma_2 grid search, given user-specified target operating characteristics (e.g., Pr(Go) Pr(NoGo) specified true parameter scenarios). search follows two-stage precompute--sweep strategy computational efficiency.

what: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
text: Generates complete set non-negative integer vectors \((x_{00}, x_{01}, x_{10}, x_{11})\) satisfy \(x_{00} + x_{01} + x_{10} + x_{11} = n\), \(n\) arm sample size.  row returned matrix corresponds one possible realisation aggregated bivariate binary counts defined Appendix A1.1 reference.  enumeration prerequisite exact scenario assessment (operating characteristics) pbayesdecisionprob2bin.

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
code: allmultinom(n)

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
text: n single non-negative integer giving arm sample size.

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
text: integer matrix \(\binom{n+3}{3}\) rows 4 columns named x00, x01, x10, x11. row distinct non-negative integer solution \(x_{00} + x_{01} + x_{10} + x_{11} = n\). Rows ordered x00 (ascending), x10 (ascending), x01 (ascending).

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
text: number non-negative integer solutions \(x_{00} + x_{01} + x_{10} + x_{11} = n\) stars--bars count $$\binom{n+3}{3} = \frac{(n+1)(n+2)(n+3)}{6}.$$ example, \(n = 10\) yields 286 rows \(n = 20\) yields 1771 rows.  matrix pre-allocated avoid repeated memory reallocation, making function efficient sample sizes typical rare-disease proof--concept studies. function internal computational building block used pbayesdecisionprob2bin enumerate sample space multinomial probabilities decision indicators summed computing exact operating characteristics.

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
code: # Example 1: n = 2 (smallest non-trivial case) allmultinom(2) #>       x00 x01 x10 x11 #>  [1,]   0   0   0   2 #>  [2,]   0   1   0   1 #>  [3,]   0   2   0   0 #>  [4,]   0   0   1   1 #>  [5,]   0   1   1   0 #>  [6,]   0   0   2   0 #>  [7,]   1   0   0   1 #>  [8,]   1   1   0   0 #>  [9,]   1   0   1   0 #> [10,]   2   0   0   0  # Example 2: n = 10 (typical rare-disease PoC arm size) mat <- allmultinom(10) nrow(mat)          # Should be choose(13, 3) = 286 #> [1] 286 all(rowSums(mat) == 10)  # Every row must sum to n #> [1] TRUE  # Example 3: n = 0 (edge case - only the all-zero row) allmultinom(0) #>      x00 x01 x10 x11 #> [1,]   0   0   0   0  # Example 4: Verify column names colnames(allmultinom(5)) #> [1] "x00" "x01" "x10" "x11"  # Example 5: Row counts match the stars-and-bars formula n <- 15L mat <- allmultinom(n) nrow(mat) == choose(n + 3L, 3L)  # Should be TRUE #> [1] TRUE

what: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
text: Computes optimal Go threshold \(\gamma_1\) NoGo threshold \(\gamma_2\) single binary endpoint searching fine grid candidate values.  search follows two-stage approach: posterior predictive probabilities precomputed every possible outcome pair \((y_1, y_2)\), operating characteristics candidate \(\gamma\) obtained fast weighted summation without additional probability evaluations.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
code: getgamma1bin(   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   theta.TV = NULL,   theta.MAV = NULL,   theta.NULL = NULL,   pi1,   pi2 = NULL,   target_go,   target_nogo,   crit_go = "<",   crit_nogo = "<",   sel_go = "smallest",   sel_nogo = "largest",   n1,   n2,   a1,   a2,   b1,   b2,   z = NULL,   m1 = NULL,   m2 = NULL,   ne1 = NULL,   ne2 = NULL,   ye1 = NULL,   ye2 = NULL,   ae1 = NULL,   ae2 = NULL,   gamma_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01) )

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
text: function uses two-stage precompute--sweep strategy efficiency: Precomputation: possible outcome pairs \((y_1, y_2)\) enumerated, posterior predictive probability \(g(y_1, y_2)\) computed every pair using pbayespostpred1bin.  step independent \(\gamma\). Gamma sweep: candidate \(\gamma\) gamma_grid, Pr(Go) Pr(NoGo) computed weighted sums precomputed indicators, weights binomial probabilities pi1 pi2.  probability evaluations required stage. prob = 'posterior', two precomputation vectors used: one evaluated theta.TV (Go criterion, lower.tail = FALSE) one theta.MAV (NoGo criterion, lower.tail = TRUE).  prob = 'predictive', criteria use theta.NULL respective tail direction. optimal \(\gamma_1\) sel_go ("smallest" "largest") value gamma_grid \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) satisfies crit_go comparison target_go.  Analogously, optimal \(\gamma_2\) sel_nogo value satisfying crit_nogo target_nogo.

what: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
text: Computes optimal Go threshold \(\gamma_1\) NoGo threshold \(\gamma_2\) two binary endpoints searching two-dimensional grid candidate value pairs.  search follows two-stage precompute--sweep strategy: region probabilities \(\hat{g}_{Go,ij}\) \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,ij}\) precomputed every possible multinomial outcome combination \((x_t, x_c)\), operating characteristics \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\) pair obtained fast weighted summation without additional Dirichlet draws.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
code: getgamma2bin(   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   GoRegions,   NoGoRegions,   pi_t1,   pi_t2,   rho_t,   pi_c1 = NULL,   pi_c2 = NULL,   rho_c = NULL,   target_go,   target_nogo,   crit_go = "<",   crit_nogo = "<",   sel_go = "smallest",   sel_nogo = "largest",   n1,   n2,   a1_00,   a1_01,   a1_10,   a1_11,   a2_00,   a2_01,   a2_10,   a2_11,   theta.TV1 = NULL,   theta.MAV1 = NULL,   theta.TV2 = NULL,   theta.MAV2 = NULL,   theta.NULL1 = NULL,   theta.NULL2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL,   m2 = NULL,   z00 = NULL,   z01 = NULL,   z10 = NULL,   z11 = NULL,   xe1_00 = NULL,   xe1_01 = NULL,   xe1_10 = NULL,   xe1_11 = NULL,   xe2_00 = NULL,   xe2_01 = NULL,   xe2_10 = NULL,   xe2_11 = NULL,   ae1 = NULL,   ae2 = NULL,   nMC = 1000L,   method = "Exact",   nsim = 10000L,   gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01) )

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
text: prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. GoRegions integer vector region indices (subset 1:9) constitute Go region.  9 regions defined cross-classification two treatment effects \((\theta_1, \theta_2)\) relative (TV1, MAV1) (TV2, MAV2): region \(k = (z_1 - 1) \times 3 + z_2\) \(z_i = 1\) \(\theta_i > TV_i\), \(z_i = 2\) \(MAV_i < \theta_i \le TV_i\), \(z_i = 3\) \(\theta_i \le MAV_i\). NoGoRegions integer vector region indices (subset 1:9) constitute NoGo region. Must disjoint GoRegions. pi_t1 numeric scalar (0, 1) giving true treatment response probability Endpoint 1 scenario used search optimal thresholds. pi_t2 numeric scalar (0, 1) giving true treatment response probability Endpoint 2. rho_t numeric scalar giving within-arm correlation treatment arm. pi_c1 numeric scalar (0, 1) giving true control response probability Endpoint 1.  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. pi_c2 numeric scalar (0, 1) giving true control response probability Endpoint 2.  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. rho_c numeric scalar giving within-arm correlation control arm.  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. target_go numeric scalar (0, 1) giving target value \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) used select optimal \(\gamma_1\).  candidate \(\gamma_1\), worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) \(\gamma_2\) gamma2_grid compared target_go using operator specified crit_go. target_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1) giving target value \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) used select optimal \(\gamma_2\).  candidate \(\gamma_2\), worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) \(\gamma_1\) gamma1_grid compared target_nogo using operator specified crit_nogo. crit_go character string specifying comparison operator applied \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) searching \(\gamma_1\).  Must one "<", "<=", ">", ">=".  Default "<". crit_nogo character string specifying comparison operator applied \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) searching \(\gamma_2\).  Must one "<", "<=", ">", ">=".  Default "<". sel_go character string specifying whether select "smallest" "largest" value gamma1_grid satisfying crit_go criterion. Default "smallest". sel_nogo character string specifying whether select "smallest" "largest" value gamma2_grid satisfying crit_nogo criterion. Default "largest". n1 positive integer giving number patients group 1 (treatment arm) PoC trial. n2 positive integer giving number patients group 2 (control arm) PoC trial. a1_00 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (0,0) response pattern group 1. a1_01 positive numeric scalar; see a1_00. a1_10 positive numeric scalar; see a1_00. a1_11 positive numeric scalar; see a1_00. a2_00 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (0,0) response pattern group 2. a2_01 positive numeric scalar; see a2_00. a2_10 positive numeric scalar; see a2_00. a2_11 positive numeric scalar; see a2_00. theta.TV1 numeric scalar giving TV threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta.MAV1 numeric scalar giving MAV threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta.TV2 numeric scalar giving TV threshold Endpoint 2.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta.MAV2 numeric scalar giving MAV threshold Endpoint 2.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta.NULL1 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. theta.NULL2 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 2.  Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m1 positive integer giving future sample size group 1.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. m2 positive integer giving future sample size group 2.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. z00 non-negative integer giving hypothetical control count pattern (0,0).  Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. z01 non-negative integer; see z00. z10 non-negative integer; see z00. z11 non-negative integer; see z00. xe1_00 non-negative integer giving external group 1 count pattern (0,0).  Required design = 'external' external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. xe1_01 non-negative integer; see xe1_00. xe1_10 non-negative integer; see xe1_00. xe1_11 non-negative integer; see xe1_00. xe2_00 non-negative integer giving external group 2 count pattern (0,0).  Required design = 'external' external control data used; otherwise NULL. xe2_01 non-negative integer; see xe2_00. xe2_10 non-negative integer; see xe2_00. xe2_11 non-negative integer; see xe2_00. ae1 numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external group 1 data.  Required external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. ae2 numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external group 2 data.  Required external control data used; otherwise NULL. nMC positive integer giving number Dirichlet draws used evaluate region probabilities count combination Stage 1.  Default 1000L. method character string specifying computation method passed pbayespostpred2bin internally. Must 'Exact' (default) 'MC'. nsim positive integer giving number outer Monte Carlo samples used method = 'MC'.  Default 10000L. gamma1_grid numeric vector candidate Go threshold values (0, 1) search .  Defaults seq(0.01, 0.99, = 0.01). gamma2_grid numeric vector candidate NoGo threshold values (0, 1) search .  Defaults seq(0.01, 0.99, = 0.01).

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
text: list class getgamma2bin following elements: gamma1 Optimal Go threshold selected gamma1_grid according crit_go sel_go.  NA value satisfies criterion. gamma2 Optimal NoGo threshold selected gamma2_grid according crit_nogo sel_nogo.  NA value satisfies criterion. PrGo_at_gamma Pr(Go) evaluated (gamma1, gamma2).  NA either threshold NA. PrNoGo_at_gamma Pr(NoGo) evaluated (gamma1, gamma2).  NA either threshold NA. gamma1_grid candidate Go threshold grid used. gamma2_grid candidate NoGo threshold grid used. PrGo_grid Numeric matrix dimensions length(gamma1_grid) x length(gamma2_grid) giving Pr(Go) grid point. PrNoGo_grid Numeric matrix dimensions giving Pr(NoGo) grid point.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
text: function uses two-stage precompute--sweep strategy pbayesdecisionprob2bin. Stage 1 (precomputation): pbayespostpred2bin called every possible multinomial outcome combination \((x_t, x_c)\) enumerated allmultinom. resulting 9-element region probability vector summed GoRegions NoGoRegions obtain \(\hat{g}_{Go,ij}\) \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,ij}\), Go/NoGo probabilities combination, independent decision thresholds \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\). Stage 2 (gamma sweep): pair \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\) two-dimensional grid, operating characteristics computed : $$\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \sum_{,j} w_{ij}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{Go,ij} \ge \gamma_1,\;   \hat{g}_{NoGo,ij} < \gamma_2\right]$$ $$\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) = \sum_{,j} w_{ij}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{NoGo,ij} \ge \gamma_2,\;   \hat{g}_{Go,ij} < \gamma_1\right]$$ \(w_{ij} = P(X_t = x_{t,}) \times P(X_c = x_{c,j})\) multinomial probability weights.  additional Dirichlet draws required step. Stage 3 (optimal threshold selection): candidate \(\gamma_1\), maximum \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) \(\gamma_2\) gamma2_grid computed; optimal \(\gamma_1\) chosen sel_go value satisfying crit_go comparison target_go.  Analogously, optimal \(\gamma_2\) sel_nogo value maximum \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) \(\gamma_1\) satisfies crit_nogo target_nogo.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
code: # Example 1: Controlled design, posterior probability # gamma1: smallest gamma1 s.t. max_{gamma2} Pr(Go)   < 0.05 # gamma2: largest  gamma2 s.t. max_{gamma1} Pr(NoGo) < 0.20 if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ getgamma2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   pi_t1 = 0.15, pi_t2 = 0.20, rho_t = 0.0,   pi_c1 = 0.15, pi_c2 = 0.20, rho_c = 0.0,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',   sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 10L, n2 = 10L,   a1_00 = 0.25, a1_01 = 0.25, a1_10 = 0.25, a1_11 = 0.25,   a2_00 = 0.25, a2_01 = 0.25, a2_10 = 0.25, a2_11 = 0.25,   theta.TV1   = 0.15, theta.MAV1 = 0.10,   theta.TV2   = 0.15, theta.MAV2 = 0.10,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,   xe1_00 = NULL, xe1_01 = NULL, xe1_10 = NULL, xe1_11 = NULL,   xe2_00 = NULL, xe2_01 = NULL, xe2_10 = NULL, xe2_11 = NULL,   ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   nMC = 100L, method = 'Exact',   gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01) ) } # what: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
text: Computes optimal Go threshold \(\gamma_1\) NoGo threshold \(\gamma_2\) two continuous endpoints searching two-dimensional grid candidate value pairs.  search follows two-stage simulate--sweep strategy: nsim bivariate datasets generated specified scenario parameters, region probabilities \(\hat{g}_{Go,}\) \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,}\) computed replicate via pbayespostpred2cont, operating characteristics \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\) pair obtained fast proportion-based evaluation without additional simulation.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
code: getgamma2cont(   nsim = 10000L,   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   prior = "vague",   GoRegions,   NoGoRegions,   mu1,   Sigma1,   mu2 = NULL,   Sigma2 = NULL,   target_go,   target_nogo,   crit_go = "<",   crit_nogo = "<",   sel_go = "smallest",   sel_nogo = "largest",   n1,   n2 = NULL,   theta.TV1 = NULL,   theta.MAV1 = NULL,   theta.TV2 = NULL,   theta.MAV2 = NULL,   theta.NULL1 = NULL,   theta.NULL2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL,   m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL,   nu01 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL,   Lambda01 = NULL,   kappa02 = NULL,   nu02 = NULL,   mu02 = NULL,   Lambda02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL,   ne2 = NULL,   alpha01e = NULL,   alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL,   ybar_e2 = NULL,   Se1 = NULL,   Se2 = NULL,   nMC = NULL,   method = "MC",   gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed = NULL )

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
text: nsim positive integer giving number Monte Carlo datasets simulate.  Default 10000L. prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. prior character string specifying prior distribution. Must 'vague' 'N-Inv-Wishart'. GoRegions integer vector region indices (subset 1:9) constitute Go region.  9 regions defined cross-classification two treatment effects \((\theta_1, \theta_2)\) relative (TV1, MAV1) (TV2, MAV2): region \(k = (z_1 - 1) \times 3 + z_2\) \(z_i = 1\) \(\theta_i > TV_i\), \(z_i = 2\) \(MAV_i < \theta_i \le TV_i\), \(z_i = 3\) \(\theta_i \le MAV_i\). NoGoRegions integer vector region indices (subset 1:9) constitute NoGo region. Must disjoint GoRegions. mu1 length-2 numeric vector giving true bivariate mean group 1 (treatment arm) scenario used search optimal thresholds. Sigma1 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving true within-arm covariance treatment arm. mu2 length-2 numeric vector giving true bivariate mean group 2 (control arm).  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. Sigma2 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving true within-arm covariance control arm.  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. target_go numeric scalar (0, 1) giving target value \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) used select optimal \(\gamma_1\).  candidate \(\gamma_1\), worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) \(\gamma_2\) gamma2_grid compared target_go using operator specified crit_go. target_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1) giving target value \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) used select optimal \(\gamma_2\).  candidate \(\gamma_2\), worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) \(\gamma_1\) gamma1_grid compared target_nogo using operator specified crit_nogo. crit_go character string specifying comparison operator applied \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) searching \(\gamma_1\).  Must one "<", "<=", ">", ">=".  Default "<". crit_nogo character string specifying comparison operator applied \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) searching \(\gamma_2\).  Must one "<", "<=", ">", ">=".  Default "<". sel_go character string specifying whether select "smallest" "largest" value gamma1_grid satisfying crit_go criterion. Default "smallest". sel_nogo character string specifying whether select "smallest" "largest" value gamma2_grid satisfying crit_nogo criterion. Default "largest". n1 positive integer giving number patients group 1 (treatment arm) PoC trial. n2 positive integer giving number patients group 2 (control arm) PoC trial.  Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. theta.TV1 numeric scalar giving TV threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta.MAV1 numeric scalar giving MAV threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta.TV2 numeric scalar giving TV threshold Endpoint 2.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta.MAV2 numeric scalar giving MAV threshold Endpoint 2.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta.NULL1 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. theta.NULL2 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 2.  Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m1 positive integer giving future sample size group 1.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. m2 positive integer giving future sample size group 2.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. kappa01 Positive numeric scalar.  NIW prior hyperparameter \(\kappa_{01}\) group 1.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL. nu01 Positive numeric scalar.  NIW prior degrees freedom \(\nu_{01}\) group 1.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL. mu01 Length-2 numeric vector.  NIW prior mean \(\mu_{01}\) group 1.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL. Lambda01 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix.  NIW prior scale matrix \(\Lambda_{01}\) group 1.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL. kappa02 Positive numeric scalar; see kappa01. group 2. nu02 Positive numeric scalar; see nu01. group 2. mu02 Length-2 numeric vector; see mu01.  group 2. May required vague prior uncontrolled design; see pbayesdecisionprob2cont. Lambda02 2x2 matrix; see Lambda01. group 2. r positive numeric scalar giving power prior weight group 2 design = 'uncontrolled' prior = 'vague'.  Otherwise NULL. ne1 positive integer giving external treatment sample size.  Required design = 'external' external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. ne2 positive integer giving external control sample size.  Required design = 'external' external control data used; otherwise NULL. alpha01e numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external treatment data.  Required external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. alpha02e numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external control data.  Required external control data used; otherwise NULL. ybar_e1 length-2 numeric vector.  External treatment sample mean.  Required external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. ybar_e2 length-2 numeric vector.  External control sample mean.  Required external control data used; otherwise NULL. Se1 2x2 numeric matrix.  External treatment sum--squares matrix.  Required external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. Se2 2x2 numeric matrix.  External control sum--squares matrix.  Required external control data used; otherwise NULL. nMC positive integer giving number Monte Carlo draws passed pbayespostpred2cont method = 'MC'.  Set NULL method = 'MM'.  Default NULL. method character string specifying computation method passed pbayespostpred2cont.  Must 'MC' (default) 'MM'. gamma1_grid numeric vector candidate Go threshold values (0, 1) search .  Defaults seq(0.01, 0.99, = 0.01). gamma2_grid numeric vector candidate NoGo threshold values (0, 1) search .  Defaults seq(0.01, 0.99, = 0.01). seed integer value reproducible random number generation.  Default NULL (seed set).

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
text: list class getgamma2cont following elements: gamma1 Optimal Go threshold selected gamma1_grid according crit_go sel_go.  NA value satisfies criterion. gamma2 Optimal NoGo threshold selected gamma2_grid according crit_nogo sel_nogo.  NA value satisfies criterion. PrGo_at_gamma Pr(Go) evaluated (gamma1, gamma2).  NA either threshold NA. PrNoGo_at_gamma Pr(NoGo) evaluated (gamma1, gamma2).  NA either threshold NA. gamma1_grid candidate Go threshold grid used. gamma2_grid candidate NoGo threshold grid used. PrGo_grid Numeric matrix dimensions length(gamma1_grid) x length(gamma2_grid) giving Pr(Go) grid point. PrNoGo_grid Numeric matrix dimensions giving Pr(NoGo) grid point.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
text: function uses two-stage simulate--sweep strategy: Stage 1 (simulation precomputation): nsim bivariate datasets generated drawing \(N_2(\mu_1, \Sigma_1)\) (\(N_2(\mu_2, \Sigma_2)\) controlled/external designs).  Sufficient statistics \((\bar{y}_{1,}, S_{1,})\) computed replicate, pbayespostpred2cont called vectorised mode return \(nsim \times 9\) matrix region probabilities. probabilities summed GoRegions NoGoRegions obtain \(\hat{g}_{Go,}\) \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,}\) replicate, independent decision thresholds \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\). Stage 2 (gamma sweep): pair \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\) two-dimensional grid, operating characteristics computed : $$\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \frac{1}{n_{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{Go,} \ge \gamma_1,\;   \hat{g}_{NoGo,} < \gamma_2\right]$$ $$\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) = \frac{1}{n_{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{NoGo,} \ge \gamma_2,\;   \hat{g}_{Go,} < \gamma_1\right]$$ additional Monte Carlo draws required step. Stage 3 (optimal threshold selection): candidate \(\gamma_1\), maximum \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) \(\gamma_2\) gamma2_grid computed; optimal \(\gamma_1\) chosen sel_go value satisfying crit_go comparison target_go.  Analogously, optimal \(\gamma_2\) sel_nogo value maximum \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) \(\gamma_1\) satisfies crit_nogo target_nogo.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
code: # Example 1: Controlled design, posterior probability, vague prior # Scenario: mu1 - mu2 = c(5, 1) at MAV boundary (intermediate effect) # gamma1: smallest gamma1 s.t. max_{gamma2} Pr(Go)   < 0.05 # gamma2: largest  gamma2 s.t. max_{gamma1} Pr(NoGo) < 0.20 if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ Sigma <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) getgamma2cont(   nsim = 1000L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   prior = 'vague',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   mu1 = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma1 = Sigma,   mu2 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma2 = Sigma,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',   sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 30L, n2 = 30L,   theta.TV1 = 10.0, theta.MAV1 = 5.0,   theta.TV2 = 2.0,  theta.MAV2 = 1.0,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,   kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',   gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed = 1L ) } # }  # Example 2: Controlled design, posterior probability, N-Inv-Wishart prior if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ Sigma  <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) Lambda <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) getgamma2cont(   nsim = 1000L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   mu1 = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma1 = Sigma,   mu2 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma2 = Sigma,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',   sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 30L, n2 = 30L,   theta.TV1 = 10.0, theta.MAV1 = 5.0,   theta.TV2 = 2.0,  theta.MAV2 = 1.0,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = 0.1, nu01 = 4.0, mu01 = c(0.0, 1.0),  Lambda01 = Lambda,   kappa02 = 0.1, nu02 = 4.0, mu02 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Lambda02 = Lambda,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',   gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed = 1L ) } # }  # Example 3: Uncontrolled design, posterior probability, vague prior if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ Sigma <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) getgamma2cont(   nsim = 1000L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',   prior = 'vague',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   mu1 = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma1 = Sigma,   mu2 = NULL, Sigma2 = NULL,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',   sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 30L, n2 = NULL,   theta.TV1 = 10.0, theta.MAV1 = 5.0,   theta.TV2 = 2.0,  theta.MAV2 = 1.0,   theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,   m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,   kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Lambda02 = NULL,   r = 1.0,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = NULL, method = 'MM',   gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed = 1L ) } # }  # Example 4: Controlled design, predictive probability, vague prior # Note: prob = 'predictive' returns 4 regions; GoRegions = 1L (both # endpoints exceed NULL), NoGoRegions = 4L (both endpoints below NULL) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ Sigma <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) getgamma2cont(   nsim = 1000L, prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled',   prior = 'vague',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 4L,   mu1 = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma1 = Sigma,   mu2 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma2 = Sigma,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',   sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',   n1 = 30L, n2 = 30L,   theta.TV1 = NULL, theta.MAV1 = NULL,   theta.TV2 = NULL, theta.MAV2 = NULL,   theta.NULL1 = 5.0, theta.NULL2 = 1.0,   m1 = 100L, m2 = 100L,   kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,   kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,   ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,   nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',   gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed = 1L ) } # }

what: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
text: Converts parameterization \((\pi_{k1}, \pi_{k2}, \rho_k)\) four-cell joint probability vector \((p_{k,00}, p_{k,01}, p_{k,10}, p_{k,11})\) bivariate binary outcome arm \(k\).  conversion uses standard moment-matching identity Pearson correlation two Bernoulli variables, checks requested correlation feasible supplied marginal rates.

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
code: getjointbin(pi1, pi2, rho, tol = 1e-10)

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
text: pi1 single numeric value \((0, 1)\) giving marginal response probability Endpoint 1. pi2 single numeric value \((0, 1)\) giving marginal response probability Endpoint 2. rho single numeric value giving Pearson correlation Endpoint 1 Endpoint 2.  Must lie within feasible range \([\rho_{\min}, \rho_{\max}]\) determined pi1 pi2 (see Details).  Use rho = 0 independence. tol single positive numeric value specifying tolerance used checking whether rho lies within feasible range whether resulting probabilities non-negative.  Default 1e-10.

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
text: named numeric vector length 4 elements p00, p01, p10, p11, p_lm = Pr(Endpoint 1 = l, Endpoint 2 = m). elements non-negative sum 1.

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
text: bivariate binary outcome \((Y_1, Y_2)\) marginal success probabilities \(\pi_1 = \Pr(Y_1 = 1)\) \(\pi_2 = \Pr(Y_2 = 1)\), Pearson correlation $$   \rho = \frac{p_{11} - \pi_1 \pi_2}              {\sqrt{\pi_1 (1-\pi_1) \pi_2 (1-\pi_2)}}. $$ Solving \(p_{11}\) gives $$p_{11} = \rho \sqrt{\pi_1(1-\pi_1)\pi_2(1-\pi_2)} + \pi_1 \pi_2,$$ remaining probabilities follow: $$p_{10} = \pi_1 - p_{11}, \quad       p_{01} = \pi_2 - p_{11}, \quad       p_{00} = 1 - p_{10} - p_{01} - p_{11}.$$ \(p_{11}\) keep four cell probabilities \([0, 1]\), correlation must satisfy $$   \rho_{\min} = \frac{\max(0,\, \pi_1 + \pi_2 - 1) - \pi_1 \pi_2}                     {\sqrt{\pi_1(1-\pi_1)\pi_2(1-\pi_2)}}   \le \rho \le   \frac{\min(\pi_1, \pi_2) - \pi_1 \pi_2}        {\sqrt{\pi_1(1-\pi_1)\pi_2(1-\pi_2)}}   = \rho_{\max}. $$ function raises error rho falls outside range (subject tol).

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
code: # Example 1: Independent endpoints (rho = 0) getjointbin(pi1 = 0.3, pi2 = 0.4, rho = 0.0) #>  p00  p01  p10  p11  #> 0.42 0.28 0.18 0.12   # Example 2: Positive correlation getjointbin(pi1 = 0.3, pi2 = 0.4, rho = 0.3) #>       p00       p01       p10       p11  #> 0.4873498 0.2126502 0.1126502 0.1873498   # Example 3: Negative correlation getjointbin(pi1 = 0.3, pi2 = 0.4, rho = -0.2) #>        p00        p01        p10        p11  #> 0.37510011 0.32489989 0.22489989 0.07510011   # Example 4: Verify cell probabilities sum to 1 p <- getjointbin(pi1 = 0.25, pi2 = 0.35, rho = 0.1) sum(p)  # Should be 1 #> [1] 1  # Example 5: Verify marginal recovery p <- getjointbin(pi1 = 0.25, pi2 = 0.35, rho = 0.1) p["p10"] + p["p11"]  # Should equal 0.25 (pi1) #>  p10  #> 0.25  p["p01"] + p["p11"]  # Should equal 0.35 (pi2) #>  p01  #> 0.35

what: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
text: Evaluates operating characteristics (Go, NoGo, Gray probabilities) binary-outcome clinical trials Bayesian framework enumerating possible trial outcomes. function supports controlled, uncontrolled, external-control designs.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
code: pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   theta.TV = NULL,   theta.MAV = NULL,   theta.NULL = NULL,   gamma1,   gamma2,   pi1,   pi2,   n1,   n2,   a1,   a2,   b1,   b2,   z = NULL,   m1 = NULL,   m2 = NULL,   ne1 = NULL,   ne2 = NULL,   ye1 = NULL,   ye2 = NULL,   ae1 = NULL,   ae2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE,   Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE )

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
text: prob character string specifying probability type decision-making. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. theta.TV numeric scalar giving target value (TV) threshold used Go decision prob = 'posterior'. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta.MAV numeric scalar giving minimum acceptable value (MAV) threshold used NoGo decision prob = 'posterior'. Must satisfy theta.TV > theta.MAV. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta.NULL numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold used Go NoGo decisions prob = 'predictive'. Set NULL prob = 'posterior'. gamma1 numeric scalar (0, 1) giving minimum posterior/predictive probability required Go decision. Typically 0.8 higher. gamma2 numeric scalar (0, 1) giving maximum posterior/predictive probability triggers NoGo decision. Must satisfy gamma2 < gamma1. pi1 numeric value vector giving true response probability(s) group 1 (treatment) used evaluate operating characteristics. element must (0, 1). pi2 numeric value vector giving true response probability(s) group 2 (control). design = 'uncontrolled', parameter used calculations must supplied; excluded output. supplied vector, must length pi1. n1 positive integer giving number patients group 1 proof--concept (PoC) trial. n2 positive integer giving number patients group 2 PoC trial (also used hypothetical control size uncontrolled design). a1 positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter (alpha) prior Beta distribution group 1. a2 positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter (alpha) prior Beta distribution group 2. b1 positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter (beta) prior Beta distribution group 1. b2 positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter (beta) prior Beta distribution group 2. z non-negative integer giving hypothetical control responder count. Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. m1 positive integer giving future sample size group 1. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m2 positive integer giving future sample size group 2. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. ne1 positive integer giving number patients group 1 external data set. Required design = 'external'; otherwise NULL. ne2 positive integer giving number patients group 2 external data set. Required design = 'external'; otherwise NULL. ye1 non-negative integer giving number responders group 1 external data set. Required design = 'external'; otherwise NULL. ye2 non-negative integer giving number responders group 2 external data set. Required design = 'external'; otherwise NULL. ae1 numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight group 1. Required design = 'external'; otherwise NULL. ae2 numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight group 2. Required design = 'external'; otherwise NULL. error_if_Miss logical scalar; TRUE (default), function stops error Miss probability > 0, prompting reconsideration thresholds. Gray_inc_Miss logical scalar; TRUE, Miss probability added Gray probability. FALSE (default), Miss reported separately. Active error_if_Miss = FALSE.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
text: data frame one row per pi1 scenario columns: pi1 True treatment response probability. pi2 True control response probability (omitted uncontrolled design). Go Probability making Go decision. Gray Probability making Gray (inconclusive) decision. NoGo Probability making NoGo decision. Miss (Optional) Probability Go NoGo criteria simultaneously met. Included error_if_Miss = FALSE Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE. returned object S3 class pbayesdecisionprob1bin associated print method.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
text: Operating characteristics computed exact enumeration: possible outcome pairs \((y_1, y_2)\) \(y_1 \\{0,\ldots,n_1\}\) \(y_2 \\{0,\ldots,n_2\}\) (fixed \(z\) uncontrolled) evaluated. pair, pbayespostpred1bin computes posterior predictive probability thresholds (TV/MAV NULL). Outcomes classified Go, NoGo, Miss, Gray: Go: \(P(\mathrm{Go}) \ge \gamma_1\) \(P(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \gamma_2\) NoGo: \(P(\mathrm{Go}) < \gamma_1\) \(P(\mathrm{NoGo}) \ge \gamma_2\) Miss: Go NoGo criteria met simultaneously Gray: neither Go NoGo criteria met outcome weighted binomial probability true rates.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
code: # Example 1: Controlled design with posterior probability pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   theta.TV = 0.4, theta.MAV = 0.2, theta.NULL = NULL,   gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.2,   pi1 = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8), pi2 = rep(0.2, 4),   n1 = 12, n2 = 12,   a1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   z = NULL, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior #>   Design           : controlled #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 0.4, MAV = 0.2 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.2 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 12, n2 = 12 #>   Prior (Beta)     : a1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5 #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi1 pi2     Go   Gray   NoGo #>  0.2 0.2 0.0004 0.0098 0.9898 #>  0.4 0.2 0.0277 0.1405 0.8318 #>  0.6 0.2 0.2224 0.3427 0.4349 #>  0.8 0.2 0.6559 0.2549 0.0892 #> ------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 2: Uncontrolled design with hypothetical control pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',   theta.TV = 0.30, theta.MAV = 0.15, theta.NULL = NULL,   gamma1 = 0.75, gamma2 = 0.25,   pi1 = c(0.3, 0.5, 0.7), pi2 = rep(0.33, 3),   n1 = 15, n2 = 15,   a1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   z = 5, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior #>   Design           : uncontrolled #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 0.3, MAV = 0.15 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.75 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.25 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 15, n2 = 15 #>   Prior (Beta)     : a1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5 #>   Uncontrolled     : z = 5 #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi1     Go   Gray   NoGo #>  0.3 0.0000 0.0036 0.9963 #>  0.5 0.0176 0.1333 0.8491 #>  0.7 0.2969 0.4248 0.2784 #> ------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 3: Posterior predictive probability for controlled design pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled',   theta.TV = NULL, theta.MAV = NULL, theta.NULL = 0,   gamma1 = 0.9, gamma2 = 0.3,   pi1 = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8), pi2 = rep(0.2, 4),   n1 = 12, n2 = 12,   a1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   z = NULL, m1 = 30, m2 = 30,   ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, ye1 = NULL, ye2 = NULL, ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : predictive #>   Design           : controlled #>   Threshold(s)     : NULL = 0 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.9 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.3 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 12, n2 = 12 #>   Prior (Beta-bin) : a1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5 #>   Future trial     : m1 = 30, m2 = 30 #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi1 pi2     Go   Gray   NoGo #>  0.2 0.2 0.0518 0.1672 0.7810 #>  0.4 0.2 0.3196 0.3429 0.3375 #>  0.6 0.2 0.7302 0.2007 0.0691 #>  0.8 0.2 0.9638 0.0324 0.0038 #> ------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 4: External design with 50 percent power prior borrowing pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external',   theta.TV = 0.4, theta.MAV = 0.2, theta.NULL = NULL,   gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.2,   pi1 = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8), pi2 = rep(0.2, 4),   n1 = 12, n2 = 12,   a1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5,   z = NULL, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   ne1 = 15, ne2 = 15, ye1 = 6, ye2 = 4, ae1 = 0.5, ae2 = 0.5,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior #>   Design           : external #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 0.4, MAV = 0.2 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.2 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 12, n2 = 12 #>   Prior (Beta)     : a1 = 0.5, a2 = 0.5, b1 = 0.5, b2 = 0.5 #>   External data    : ne1 = 15, ne2 = 15, ye1 = 6, ye2 = 4, ae1 = 0.5, ae2 = 0.5 #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi1 pi2     Go   Gray   NoGo #>  0.2 0.2 0.0000 0.0023 0.9977 #>  0.4 0.2 0.0003 0.0756 0.9241 #>  0.6 0.2 0.0104 0.3742 0.6154 #>  0.8 0.2 0.1145 0.6966 0.1889 #> ------------------------------------------------------------

what: Calculate Go, NoGo, and Gray Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Continuous Endpoint Under the Bayesian Framework Using Two Metrics — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
title: Calculate Go, NoGo, and Gray Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Continuous Endpoint Under the Bayesian Framework Using Two Metrics — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
text: function calculates Go, NoGo, Gray probabilities continuous outcome clinical trials using Bayesian framework. function evaluates operating characteristics computing probability making type decision (Go, NoGo, Gray) across different true mean values Monte Carlo simulation. function supports controlled, uncontrolled, external control designs.

title: Calculate Go, NoGo, and Gray Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Continuous Endpoint Under the Bayesian Framework Using Two Metrics — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
code: pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim,   prob,   design,   prior,   CalcMethod,   theta.TV,   theta.MAV,   theta.NULL,   nMC = NULL,   gamma1,   gamma2,   n1,   n2,   m1,   m2,   kappa01,   kappa02,   nu01,   nu02,   mu01,   mu02,   sigma01,   sigma02,   mu1,   mu2,   sigma1,   sigma2,   r = NULL,   ne1 = NULL,   ne2 = NULL,   alpha01 = NULL,   alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL,   bar.ye2 = NULL,   se1 = NULL,   se2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE,   Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE,   seed )

title: Calculate Go, NoGo, and Gray Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Continuous Endpoint Under the Bayesian Framework Using Two Metrics — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
text: nsim positive integer representing number Monte Carlo simulations evaluating operating characteristics. prob character string specifying type probability use decision-making. Options 'posterior' posterior probability 'predictive' posterior predictive probability. design character string specifying type trial design. Options 'controlled' randomized controlled trials, 'uncontrolled' single-arm studies informative priors, 'external' designs incorporating external data power priors. prior character string specifying prior distribution use. Options 'vague' vague prior 'N-Inv-Chisq' Normal-Inverse-Chi-squared prior. CalcMethod character string specifying calculation method computing probabilities. Options 'NI' numerical integration, 'MC' Monte Carlo simulation, 'MM' Moment-Matching approximation. theta.TV numeric value representing target value threshold calculating Go probability prob = 'posterior'. represents minimum clinically meaningful treatment effect. theta.MAV numeric value representing minimum acceptable value threshold calculating NoGo probability prob = 'posterior'. represents minimum effect size treatment worth pursuing. theta.NULL numeric value representing null hypothesis value calculating Go/NoGo probabilities prob = 'predictive'. nMC positive integer representing number Monte Carlo samples probability calculation CalcMethod = 'MC' (required CalcMethod = 'MC'). gamma1 numeric value (0, 1) representing threshold Go decision. P(treatment effect > threshold | data) >= gamma1, decision Go. gamma2 numeric value (0, 1) representing threshold NoGo decision. P(treatment effect > threshold | data) <= gamma2, decision NoGo. Must satisfy gamma2 < gamma1. n1 positive integer representing number patients group 1 (treatment) proof--concept (PoC) trial. n2 positive integer representing number patients group 2 (control) PoC trial. uncontrolled design, represents effective sample size historical control (encoded prior). m1 positive integer representing number patients group 1 future trial (required prob = 'predictive'). m2 positive integer representing number patients group 2 future trial (required prob = 'predictive'). kappa01 positive numeric value representing prior sample size parameter group 1 prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'. kappa02 positive numeric value representing prior sample size parameter group 2 prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'. nu01 positive numeric value representing prior degrees freedom group 1 prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'. nu02 positive numeric value representing prior degrees freedom group 2 prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'. mu01 numeric value representing prior mean group 1 prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'. mu02 numeric value representing prior mean group 2 prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'. sigma01 positive numeric value representing prior scale parameter group 1 prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'. sigma02 positive numeric value representing prior scale parameter group 2 prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'. mu1 numeric value representing true mean group 1 simulation. mu2 numeric value representing true mean group 2 simulation. uncontrolled design, represents historical control mean. sigma1 positive numeric value representing true standard deviation group 1 simulation. sigma2 positive numeric value representing true standard deviation group 2 simulation. uncontrolled design, represents historical control standard deviation. r positive numeric value representing variance scaling factor allows scale hypothetical control different treatment. Specifically, sd.control = sqrt(r) * sd.treatment. Required design = 'uncontrolled'. r = 1, control treatment variance scale. ne1 positive integer representing number patients group 1 external data. Required design = 'external' external treatment data available. ne2 positive integer representing number patients group 2 external data. Required design = 'external' external control data available. alpha01 numeric value (0, 1] representing power prior scale parameter group 1. Controls degree borrowing external treatment data: 0 = borrowing, 1 = full borrowing. Required ne1 specified. alpha02 numeric value (0, 1] representing power prior scale parameter group 2. Controls degree borrowing external control data: 0 = borrowing, 1 = full borrowing. Required ne2 specified. bar.ye1 numeric value representing sample mean external data group 1. Required ne1 specified. bar.ye2 numeric value representing sample mean external data group 2. Required ne2 specified. se1 positive numeric value representing sample standard deviation external data group 1. Required ne1 specified. se2 positive numeric value representing sample standard deviation external data group 2. Required ne2 specified. error_if_Miss logical value; TRUE (default), function stops error positive Miss probability obtained, indicating poorly chosen thresholds. FALSE, function proceeds reports Miss probability based Gray_inc_Miss setting. Gray_inc_Miss logical value; TRUE, Miss probability included Gray probability (Miss reported separately). FALSE (default), Miss probability reported separate category. parameter active error_if_Miss = FALSE. seed numeric value representing seed number reproducible random number generation.

title: Calculate Go, NoGo, and Gray Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Continuous Endpoint Under the Bayesian Framework Using Two Metrics — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
text: data frame containing true means groups Go, NoGo, Gray probabilities. error_if_Miss = FALSE Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, Miss probability also included separate column. uncontrolled design, mu1 included (mu2).

title: Calculate Go, NoGo, and Gray Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Continuous Endpoint Under the Bayesian Framework Using Two Metrics — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
text: function performs Monte Carlo simulation evaluate operating characteristics : Generating random trial data based specified true parameters Computing posterior predictive probabilities simulated trial Classifying trial Go, NoGo, Gray based decision thresholds Posterior parameter calculations (mu.t1, mu.t2, sd.t1, sd.t2, nu.t1, nu.t2) fully vectorized nsim simulated datasets. pbayespostpred1cont called twice (per threshold), call receiving vectors length nsim returning vector nsim probabilities - inner loop simulation replicates required. external control designs, power priors incorporated using exact conjugate representation: Power priors normal data mathematically equivalent Normal-Inverse-Chi-squared distributions enables closed-form computation without MCMC sampling Alpha parameters control degree borrowing (0 = borrowing, 1 = full borrowing) Decision rules: Go: P(treatment effect > threshold) >= gamma1 NoGo: P(treatment effect > threshold) <= gamma2 Gray: gamma2 < P(treatment effect > threshold) < gamma1 Miss: Go NoGo criteria met simultaneously (indicates poorly chosen thresholds) Handling Miss probability: error_if_Miss = TRUE (default): Function stops error Miss probability > 0, prompting reconsideration thresholds error_if_Miss = FALSE Gray_inc_Miss = TRUE: Miss probability added Gray probability error_if_Miss = FALSE Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE: Miss probability reported separate category function can used : Controlled design: Two-arm randomized trial Uncontrolled design: Single-arm trial informative priors (historical control) External design: Incorporating historical data power priors

title: Calculate Go, NoGo, and Gray Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Continuous Endpoint Under the Bayesian Framework Using Two Metrics — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
code: # Example 1: Controlled design with vague prior and NI method # (default: error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE) pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague', CalcMethod = 'NI',   theta.TV = 1.5, theta.MAV = -0.5, theta.NULL = NULL,   nMC = NULL, gamma1 = 0.7, gamma2 = 0.2,   n1 = 15, n2 = 15, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   mu1 = 3, mu2 = 1, sigma1 = 1.2, sigma2 = 1.1,   r = NULL, ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se1 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 2 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior #>   Design           : controlled #>   Method           : Numerical Integration (NI) #>   Simulations      : 100 #>   Seed             : 2 #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 1.5, MAV = -0.5 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.7 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.2 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 15, n2 = 15 #>   True parameters  : sigma1 = 1.2, sigma2 = 1.1 #>   Prior (vague)    : (no hyperparameters) #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu1 mu2     Go   Gray   NoGo #>    3   1 0.7300 0.2700 0.0000 #> ------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 2: Uncontrolled design with informative prior pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq', CalcMethod = 'NI',   theta.TV = 1.0, theta.MAV = 0.0, theta.NULL = NULL,   nMC = NULL, gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.2,   n1 = 20, n2 = 20, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = 2, kappa02 = 20, nu01 = 5, nu02 = 20,   mu01 = 3.0, mu02 = 1.5, sigma01 = 1.5, sigma02 = 1.2,   mu1 = 3.5, mu2 = 1.5, sigma1 = 1.3, sigma2 = 1.2,   r = 1, ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se1 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 3 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior #>   Design           : uncontrolled #>   Method           : Numerical Integration (NI) #>   Simulations      : 100 #>   Seed             : 3 #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 1, MAV = 0 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.8 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.2 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 20, n2 = 20 #>   True parameters  : sigma1 = 1.3 #>   Prior (N-Inv-X2) : kappa01 = 2, kappa02 = 20, nu01 = 5, nu02 = 20 #>                      mu01 = 3, mu02 = 1.5, sigma01 = 1.5, sigma02 = 1.2 #>   Uncontrolled     : r = 1 #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu1     Go   Gray   NoGo #>  3.5 1.0000 0.0000 0.0000 #> ------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 3: External design with control data using MM approximation if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', prior = 'vague', CalcMethod = 'MM',   theta.TV = 1.0, theta.MAV = 0.0, theta.NULL = NULL,   nMC = NULL, gamma1 = 0.8, gamma2 = 0.2,   n1 = 12, n2 = 12, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   mu1 = 2, mu2 = 0, sigma1 = 1, sigma2 = 1,   r = NULL, ne1 = NULL, ne2 = 20, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = 0.5,   bar.ye1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = 0, se1 = NULL, se2 = 1,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 4 ) } # }  # Example 4: Controlled design with predictive probability pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq', CalcMethod = 'NI',   theta.TV = NULL, theta.MAV = NULL, theta.NULL = 2.0,   nMC = NULL, gamma1 = 0.75, gamma2 = 0.35,   n1 = 15, n2 = 15, m1 = 50, m2 = 50,   kappa01 = 3, kappa02 = 3, nu01 = 4, nu02 = 4,   mu01 = 3.5, mu02 = 1.5, sigma01 = 1.5, sigma02 = 1.5,   mu1 = 3.2, mu2 = 1.3, sigma1 = 1.4, sigma2 = 1.2,   r = NULL, ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se1 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 5 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : predictive #>   Design           : controlled #>   Method           : Numerical Integration (NI) #>   Simulations      : 100 #>   Seed             : 5 #>   Threshold(s)     : NULL = 2 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.75 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.35 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 15, n2 = 15 #>   True parameters  : sigma1 = 1.4, sigma2 = 1.2 #>   Prior (N-Inv-X2) : kappa01 = 3, kappa02 = 3, nu01 = 4, nu02 = 4 #>                      mu01 = 3.5, mu02 = 1.5, sigma01 = 1.5, sigma02 = 1.5 #>   Future trial     : m1 = 50, m2 = 50 #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu1 mu2     Go   Gray   NoGo #>  3.2 1.3 0.2400 0.0600 0.7000 #> ------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 5: External design with predictive probability using MC method if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'predictive', design = 'external', prior = 'vague', CalcMethod = 'MC',   theta.TV = NULL, theta.MAV = NULL, theta.NULL = 1.5,   nMC = 5000, gamma1 = 0.7, gamma2 = 0.4,   n1 = 12, n2 = 12, m1 = 30, m2 = 30,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   mu1 = 2.5, mu2 = 1.0, sigma1 = 1.3, sigma2 = 1.1,   r = NULL, ne1 = 15, ne2 = 18, alpha01 = 0.6, alpha02 = 0.7,   bar.ye1 = 2.3, bar.ye2 = 0.9, se1 = 1.2, se2 = 1.0,   error_if_Miss = FALSE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 6 ) } # }  # Example 6: Report Miss probability separately when thresholds may be suboptimal pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague', CalcMethod = 'NI',   theta.TV = 1.0, theta.MAV = 0.8, theta.NULL = NULL,   nMC = NULL, gamma1 = 0.65, gamma2 = 0.55,   n1 = 10, n2 = 10, m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,   kappa01 = NULL, kappa02 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, nu02 = NULL,   mu01 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, sigma01 = NULL, sigma02 = NULL,   mu1 = 2.5, mu2 = 1.5, sigma1 = 1.0, sigma2 = 1.0,   r = NULL, ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01 = NULL, alpha02 = NULL,   bar.ye1 = NULL, bar.ye2 = NULL, se1 = NULL, se2 = NULL,   error_if_Miss = FALSE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 7 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint) #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior #>   Design           : controlled #>   Method           : Numerical Integration (NI) #>   Simulations      : 100 #>   Seed             : 7 #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 1, MAV = 0.8 #>   Go  threshold    : gamma1 = 0.65 #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma2 = 0.55 #>   Sample size      : n1 = 10, n2 = 10 #>   True parameters  : sigma1 = 1, sigma2 = 1 #>   Prior (vague)    : (no hyperparameters) #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = FALSE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu1 mu2     Go   Gray   NoGo   Miss #>  2.5 1.5 0.3400 0.3800 0.2800 0.0000 #> ------------------------------------------------------------   # title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with Two Binary Endpoints — pbayesdecisionprob2bin
text: Evaluates operating characteristics (Go, NoGo, Gray probabilities) clinical trials two binary endpoints Bayesian framework. function supports controlled, uncontrolled, external-control designs, uses posterior probability posterior predictive probability criteria.

Ignored method = 'Exact'.  Default 10000. prob character string specifying probability type decision-making.  Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design.  Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. GoRegions integer vector specifying nine posterior regions (R1–R9) four predictive regions (R1–R4) constitute Go decision.  prob = 'posterior', valid values integers 1–9; prob = 'predictive', 1–4. common choice GoRegions = 1 (endpoints exceed TV NULL posterior/predictive, respectively). NoGoRegions integer vector specifying regions constitute NoGo decision.  common choice NoGoRegions = 9 (endpoints MAV) posterior, NoGoRegions = 4 predictive.  Must disjoint GoRegions. gamma1 numeric scalar (0, 1) giving minimum posterior/predictive probability required Go decision. gamma2 numeric scalar (0, 1) giving minimum posterior/predictive probability required NoGo decision. Unlike single-endpoint designs, gamma2 may greater , equal , less gamma1, Go NoGo regions structurally asymmetric (e.g., Go = R1 vs NoGo = R9 ) calibrated thresholds independent. pi_t1 numeric vector true treatment response probabilities Endpoint 1.  element must (0, 1). pi_t2 numeric vector true treatment response probabilities Endpoint 2. Must length pi_t1.  element must within feasible range corresponding (pi_t1, pi_t2) pair (checked via getjointbin). pi_c1 numeric vector true control response probabilities Endpoint 1.  design = 'uncontrolled', parameter used probability calculations must still supplied; retained output reference. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m2 positive integer giving future sample size group 2. Required design = 'external' external control data used; otherwise NULL. xe2_01 non-negative integer; see xe2_00. xe2_10 non-negative integer; see xe2_00. xe2_11 non-negative integer; see xe2_00. ae1 numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight group 1.
